Subclause f is nested nine layers deep with a definition at the bottom, like a pit with a smaller pit in it. You come to on the floor with the midwife counting your pulse and the meter charging your deep recovery breaths at the involuntary rate, which is, of course, higher. The room is furious on your behalf, which is a kind of oxygen too.

choices.text: Invoice UAV for the air its own document consumed

success.text: You total the reading's consumption, four hundred pages at a lungful a page, and invoice Universal Air Ventures for air consumed by their own document. Their tariff, clause forty, says consumption is chargeable to the party that causes it, and nothing in recorded history has caused more breathing than their terms of service. Legal spends eleven days looking for an exit from their own clause and pays under protest. The meter prints the credit with what witnesses later describe as a flourish.

The gallery of neighbors who came to watch you be clever now watches you hold a voucher. The midwife pours you tea and says nothing at considerable length.

text:
OXYGEN PATENT

Overnight, every meter on the street receives a firmware update called Loyalty Pricing. Sighs are up forty percent, gasps count double, and between two and four in the morning the air is described as buffering. Your meter has begun printing tiny apologies at the bottom of each receipt, in a font it clearly hopes management cannot read.

choices.text: Open every window on the street at the same hour

outcomes.text: At the agreed hour, every window on the street goes up with a sound like the neighborhood clearing its throat. Free sky pours through forty kitchens at once, and the meters can only watch, because windows predate the patent and grandfathered openings bill at zero. The street institutes a draft rota, ten windows per shift, the widow at number nine directing crosswinds like weather that owes her money. Your meter records an evening of zero consumption, prints a question mark, and stands it in the corner of the receipt like a small flag.
effects: schedule 3 r18_oxygen_patent_coalition The air is under warranty, and it is failing

outcomes.text: Window seventeen has not opened since the coronation and defends itself. You put your shoulder to the sash, the sash puts itself onto your fingers, and the street learns several words that cost extra to say while sighing. The draft happens anyway, glorious, cross ventilated, unbillable, and the neighbors cheer through it. You get a free evening of sky and a hand that will be describing that window to strangers for a week.

choices.text: Seek an injunction against Loyalty Pricing

outcomes.text: The magistrate reads the words Loyalty Pricing, reads them again, and asks UAV's counsel to whom, precisely, the loyalty belongs. Counsel explains it flows from the customer to the product, as is traditional. The injunction lands the same afternoon: prices frozen, gasps restored to single billing, the firmware wardens recalled to the depot to await instructions that never come. Your meter reverts with an audible relief it will deny in later testimony.

You are invoiced for the hearing room's air, split between parties, though UAV's half is waived under a volume discount you will never see. choices.text: Stuff the premium intake with wet laundry

success.text: Two in the morning, one ladder, one basket of wet laundry, and the premium intake swallows a fitted sheet with barely a cough. All night the street breathes whatever the sky sends, unfiltered, unimproved, and entirely unnoticed by anyone's nose. That is the interesting part, and you write it down: nobody could tell. The league disavows your methods at a volume that suggests they are keeping the finding.

You limp home to find your meter has printed the word SORRY seventeen times on one receipt. The league pays your fine from the kitty and asks you, gently, to leave the pipework to the laundry.

Word of the warranty claim travels. The choir arrives first, fined ruinously for a held note in a requiem, followed by the baker, whose yeast has been classified as forty thousand unregistered subscribers. The retired midwife adds each grievance to the claim in her delivery room handwriting, and your meter, unprompted, waives one sigh, then looks away.

choices.text: Merge every grievance into one enormous claim

outcomes.text: You staple the choir to the baker to the pigeon keeper to nine hundred smaller griefs: one claim, one docket, one spine of green thread. UAV requests three continuances in a row, which is the sound a company makes while backing up slowly. The wardens on the street are quietly reassigned to depot inventory, where the shelves do not sigh and there is nothing to fine.

text:
OXYGEN PATENT

Universal Air Ventures announces a full product recall of the atmosphere. The product, they say, has a defect, namely that people keep using it without upgrading, and all units must be returned to the depot in original packaging. The notice does not say what the original packaging of air is, but the small print suggests lungs. Compliance wardens go door to door with collection bags, and your meter prints RECALL in letters that get smaller each time, as if hoping to disappear inside the word.

choices.text: Take a paid shift at the recall depot

outcomes.text: The depot pays two cents a unit to certify returned atmosphere, and the returned atmosphere is empty jars, which you certify with a straight face for eight hours. Fourteen coins, plus a paper hat that says RECALL TEAM. Your neighbors pass the window in a queue, holding jars of used breath and their opinions, both plainly visible. At shift's end the meter prints your wages, itemized, and beneath them, unprompted, a small apology on your behalf.
effects: schedule 5 r18_oxygen_patent_compromise The air is under warranty, and it is failing

choices.text: Return the street's air one story at a time

success.text: The street complies with the recall completely, one exhale at a time. Neighbors queue to breathe their air back into the collection bags, and between breaths they tell stories, long ones, with genealogies. By the third hour the wardens understand they are hostages of procedure, their own manual forbidding them to refuse a return in progress. At dusk the recall is suspended on the grounds that the product is coming back too slowly to matter, and the street cheers, which technically returns a little more.
effects: schedule 5 r18_oxygen_patent_compromise The air is under warranty, and it is failing

fail.text: You attempt to withhold your entire breath as a matter of principle, on the doorstep, in front of witnesses. Principle lasts ninety seconds. You come around inside a collection bag, logged as returned goods, and the warden will not release you without a receiving stamp. The midwife retrieves you by signing for you as damaged inventory, and she will never, ever, let it rest.
effects: schedule 5 r18_oxygen_patent_compromise The air is under warranty, and it is failing

choices.text: Demand the recall follow its own warranty rules

outcomes.text: You read the recall notice against warranty procedure and find the sentence UAV wrote without thinking: all recalled units will be refunded at purchase price. The atmosphere has been billed by the breath for a year, and their counsel computes the liability live, in front of everyone, to the sound of a man aging. He asks for air. The room reminds him it is recalled. The withdrawal arrives by messenger before its own ink dries.
effects: schedule 5 r18_oxygen_patent_compromise The air is under warranty, and it is failing

outcomes.text: UAV amends the recall overnight to a voluntary advisory, which changes the wardens' bags not at all, only their vocabulary. The collections are now called feedback. Your procedural objection is filed under enthusiasm and scheduled for a hearing in the spring, no particular spring. The street keeps its air by keeping its doors shut, which the meters bill as indoor premium, and the kitty tin groans.
effects: schedule 5 r18_oxygen_patent_compromise The air is under warranty, and it is failing

text:
OXYGEN PATENT

At the hearing, the System appoints a standards inspector with a certified nose and a long career of not smiling. Two valves are wheeled in for a blind test, one piped Alpine at premium rates, one drawn from the stairwell outside, free where it stands. The inspector inhales each, twice, makes a note, inhales again, and stops. His nose, certified since before the patent existed, cannot tell them apart, and he would like everyone to remain calm while he says so for the record.

choices.text: Fill the gallery with neighbors who breathe both

outcomes.text: Forty neighbors sample both valves under oath and produce forty descriptions of the premium product, of which thirty nine are the word air, and one is air, but with fewer opinions. The stairwell valve receives, on average, warmer reviews. The inspector notes that the products are distinguishable only by invoice, and the phrase enters the record like a knife going into a drawer. In the corner your meter, present as an exhibit, waives a sigh nobody sighed, practicing.
effects: schedule 2 r18_oxygen_patent_reversal The air is under warranty, and it is failing

outcomes.text: You volunteer for the calibration round, forty short sniffs of alternating valves at speed while a clerk shouts the tier names. The room begins to sparkle around sniff thirty three, and you sit down without consulting yourself about it. The inspector logs your collapse as inconclusive with respect to flavor. The gallery boos the phrase, and the ushers cannot find a rule against booing a phrase.
effects: schedule 2 r18_oxygen_patent_reversal The air is under warranty, and it is failing

choices.text: Make the vice president take the blind test himself

success.text: You move that the manufacturer identify its own product, blind, for the record. The vice president of atmosphere inhales the stairwell valve and praises its structure, its finish, its unmistakable Alpine minerality, available only through certified pipe. He then inhales actual Alpine and asks whether the valve is broken. The inspector writes one sentence with no adjectives in it, and UAV's counsel requests a recess in the voice of a slow puncture.
effects: schedule 2 r18_oxygen_patent_reversal The air is under warranty, and it is failing

fail.text: UAV declines the test on the grounds that the vice president's palate is a trade secret. You expect the panel to laugh, and it does, and then it sustains the objection, because trade secrets are load bearing in this jurisdiction and laughter is not. The blind test is struck and adjourned to a room with no gallery. The inspector, leaving, sniffs the free valve once more, off the record, for personal reasons.
effects: schedule 2 r18_oxygen_patent_reversal The air is under warranty, and it is failing

choices.text: Audit the meter's logs line by line

success.text: The logs are honest, which is the surprise: every sigh timestamped, every gasp graded, and UAV's rounding always upward, always theirs, two cents at a time for a year. You claim the statutory auditor's fee, four coins, and refunds are ordered street wide. On the last page you find a column the meter invented for itself, headed sighs waived, unbilled, do not tell. The league thanks you for the refunds and side eyes the fee, since somebody always eats first at a rescue.
effects: schedule 2 r18_oxygen_patent_reversal The air is under warranty, and it is failing

fail.text: The logs defeat you around midnight, a notation system with tenses, and you fall asleep on the evidence. UAV finds you there and offers eight coins for your annotated copy, purely to see what you saw, and you are tired, and eight is eight. Their clerks read your margins and quietly fix three loopholes before the hearing resumes. The street learns who fixed them, approximately, and the kitty tin is turned to face the wall when you pass.
effects: schedule 2 r18_oxygen_patent_reversal The air is under warranty, and it is failing

text:
OXYGEN PATENT

Counsel for the league calls its final witness, your meter, carried to the stand on a cushion. Asked to state its occupation, it prints, quietly, THE COUNTING OF BREATH. Asked whether Alpine ever differed from stairwell air, it begins to hyperventilate, tape spooling in loops over the rail, and what comes out is a confession: the premium pipe has been open to the sky at both ends since installation, and the oxygen works contains one fan, facing a forest.

choices.text: Enter the meter's tape into evidence, all of it

outcomes.text: The tape runs to sixty feet and the clerk winds it onto the courtroom spindle like a man landing a fish. UAV objects that the witness was never sworn, at which the meter prints an oath of its own composition, ending in one complimentary sigh, no charge, notarize where indicated. The panel admits the whole confession, fan, forest, open pipe and all. The patent comes apart clause by clause, each one read aloud a last time, gently, before it goes.
effects: schedule 3 r18_oxygen_patent_legacy The air is under warranty, and it is failing

outcomes.text: UAV argues a meter cannot testify against its operator, then argues the meter is UAV property, then hears what it has just said. The panel adjourns to consider whether a machine can perjure itself by weeping, which is how the spooling is entered in the minutes. Mistrial hangs in the air, unbilled for once. Your meter is sequestered overnight in an evidence locker where, the bailiff reports, it prints slowly to itself until dawn.
effects: schedule 3 r18_oxygen_patent_legacy The air is under warranty, and it is failing

choices.text: Testify that sighing is a luxury you chose

outcomes.text: UAV's counsel walks you through your testimony in advance, and it is short: sighing is a luxury, you chose it, you would choose it again at these prices. Fourteen coins, eight before, six after, folded into an envelope with the smoothness of long practice. You deliver the line to the panel and to your street at the same time, the gallery being the street. On its cushion the meter prints one word, OH, and charges no one for it.

choices.text: Chip in for the meter's legal representation

outcomes.text: The hat goes around and comes back with three of your coins in it, enough to retain the smallest barrister in the building for the strangest client of his career. He objects to compound questions on the grounds that his client cannot compound, and wins. Under cross, the meter is asked why it kept the secret column, and prints, after a long spool, THE SIGHS WERE NOT THE PRODUCT, THE SIGHS WERE THE CUSTOMERS LEAVING. The gallery goes quiet in the way rooms go quiet when a machine says the true thing.

outcomes.text: Court adjourns and a warden makes a last try for the witness, on the steps, in daylight, with the whole street watching. The tug of war is brief and impolite, and you keep the meter at the cost of a thumbnail and your good coat's left elbow. The street forms an escort home, twelve neighbors deep, the meter riding in a bread basket like a small mayor. The warden files an incident report that nobody, including the incident, will ever read.

text:
OXYGEN PATENT

The verdict runs to one sentence: air remains too common to own, costs to the party who tried. Universal Air Ventures is ordered to refund every billed breath, payable in coin or in silence, and the vice president of atmosphere is last seen inhaling economically. The meter lives on the league's mantel now, retired with honors, and the retired midwife has named it Gustav, the first thing she has named in years that did not cry.

choices.text: Set up a refund advisory table, cash only

outcomes.text: You set up a table beside the refund queue, two cents on every claim to do the arithmetic, and the arithmetic is real: interest, overage, the involuntary rate, most people underclaim by half. Fourteen coins by Friday, and every customer walks away richer than they arrived, which is a strange thing to feel sideways about. The midwife pencils your name back into the ledger of first breaths, then adds an asterisk. You do not ask what the asterisk means, and she does not offer, and the asterisk stays.

choices.text: Take a caretaker shift at the oxygen works

outcomes.text: Your first caretaker shift at the oxygen works is quiet: one fan, one forest, one logbook with a pencil on a string. You oil the fan out of respect, thank the forest as per the new custom, and mark the day's air as received in full, no charge. The baker sends bread up with the shift change, the yeast being sentimental about the place. Back home on the mantel, Gustav counts your breaths out of habit and waives them all, one by one, like a man dealing out a deck he finally likes.

Your shift begins with a pigeon in the fan housing and ends with you in the blackberries below the ladder. The pigeon is fine. You are structurally fine, decoratively scratched, and the logbook now contains the entry incident, resolved, bird ungrateful. The street hears the works nearly ate its first caretaker and turns up in numbers to fit a guard screen, loudly, with opinions about ladders.

choices.text: Teach the meter to print refunds instead of bills

success.text: Reversing a meter turns out to be one screw and an apology. Gustav now prints refunds instead of bills, each stamped with a small complimentary sigh, no charge, while the queue puzzles over the novelty of enjoying a receipt. The System certifies him as the district's first instrument of disbursement. He has never been busier, and the column he once hid, sighs waived, unbilled, is now simply called the ledger.

fail.text: Gustav takes to refunding with the zeal of the converted and begins reimbursing people for breaths they never took, on the theory that they might have, had air been kinder. The System flags the generosity as fraud and sends two auditors with matching briefcases. You hide Gustav in the bread oven, unlit, where he is warm and among friends, printing quietly. The auditors stay a week, and the paperwork outlasts everyone's patience, surviving on the kitty's tea budget.

text:
RATION ALGORITHM

Six in the morning, and the depot bread queue has been alphabetized. The Office of Objective Need's Allocator feeds surnames before bodies, so the widow on queue seven, born unluckily into the letter W, has not eaten since Thursday. The machine will release her loaf once she submits proof of continued existence, which it does not accept as evidence when she says it standing right there. Two tired debuggers in inked aprons wave you over: they have a ticket queue, a kettle, and a theory.

choices.text: Scalp certified existence to the highest bidder

outcomes.text: You discover the Allocator accepts any slip stamped twice, so you stamp them twice and charge by the letter of the alphabet. The W's pay a premium, which you describe as a handling fee, and the Office quietly buys a batch to hit its throughput target, filing the precedent under flexible verification for later use on people it dislikes. Everyone eats except the people who could not afford to exist. The machine prints your receipt without comment, which somehow feels like a comment.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ration_algorithm_assembly The Allocator still wants proof you exist

choices.text: Join the debuggers and take the widow's ticket

success.text: You log defect one four one: MACHINE CANNOT SEE PEOPLE, severity high, reported by everyone. The senior debugger, who names error codes after her exes, adds you to the rota and hands you the widow's file, six inches of a woman being alive. By noon you have collected proof of continued existence for half the queue, using the radical method of looking at them. The Allocator accepts the paperwork and dispenses, and the widow eats where she stood, which is where the evidence had been all morning.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ration_algorithm_assembly The Allocator still wants proof you exist

fail.text: You reach into the dispenser chute for the widow's held loaf, and the Allocator, following procedure, closes on your wrist while requesting your proof of continued existence. The queue riots on your behalf, which is touching and unhelpful. The debuggers pry you loose, log your knuckles as supporting documentation, and add you to the rota, since you clearly have the passion.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ration_algorithm_assembly The Allocator still wants proof you exist

choices.text: File a formal defect against the feeding order

outcomes.text: You draft it in the Office's own dialect: the algorithm violates specification section nine, ALL SOULS SERVED, by defining soul alphabetically. A clerk reads it twice, goes pale in an official capacity, and stamps it RECEIVED AND REGRETTED. The queue moves for the first time since Thursday, and the debuggers start citing your ticket number like scripture.

outcomes.text: Your defect is rejected as a duplicate of a ticket filed nine years ago, which was itself closed as WORKING AS INTENDED. Objecting to that phrase earns you a citation for tone. The queue takes note that the proper channels are a loop, and its patience begins expiring alphabetically.

text:
RATION ALGORITHM

Underneath the depot, the debuggers hold ticket triage beside a boiler that also has a defect number. Tonight's queue: forty starvation reports, one misfiled birth, and the Allocator itself, which has begun printing REQUEST CLARIFY: WHAT IS A BODY in the margins of its ration slips. The senior debugger taps the margin and says the machine is not being difficult, it is being honest, which is worse. Somebody has to reproduce the bug before the Office reproduces the excuse.

choices.text: Run triage till every hunger ticket has a name

success.text: You chair the triage like a field hospital: bodies first, letters never. Each ticket gets a person attached, a neighbor who vouches, a debugger who owns it until it eats. The boiler hisses approvingly, and even the Allocator's margins change, from WHAT IS A BODY to QUERY RECEIVED, THANK YOU. It is the first thank you the Office has produced in living memory, and it came from the plumbing.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ration_algorithm_retaliation The Allocator still wants proof you exist

fail.text: Triage runs nineteen hours before you fall asleep face down on ticket two twelve and wake up stapled to it. Upstairs, someone hears the boiler room is full of unauthorized compassion and files a noise complaint. You are now on two rotas and one watchlist, and your cheek says TWO TWELVE backwards, which the debuggers agree is a promotion.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ration_algorithm_retaliation The Allocator still wants proof you exist

choices.text: Feed the machine a surname it cannot digest

outcomes.text: You register a starving man under a surname that is the letter A written seven times, and the Allocator vaults him to the front, feeds him instantly, and logs a triumph. Then you lay both slips before the depot inspector: same stomach, two fates, one spelling. The inspector stares at the evidence, then at the machine, then formally concedes that hunger is not alphabetical, a sentence the debuggers copy onto the boiler-room wall in chalk.

You have made the Allocator paranoid and the queue longer. The Office thanks you for the hardening opportunity in a memo you cannot prove is sarcastic.

choices.text: Auction the front of the alphabet

outcomes.text: You quietly sell surname changes, one stamped deed poll at a time, and by Friday the queue is full of freshly minted Abbots and Aarons. The Office pays you as well, for consulting on demand smoothing. The Allocator processes the sudden genealogy without blinking, though it prints CLARIFY: SAME BODIES? at the bottom of the day sheet. Nobody clarifies, and the W's stand where they always stood, only now behind people who used to stand beside them.

text:
RATION ALGORITHM

Retaliation arrives on official letterhead: every debugger is now required to prove continued existence weekly, in person, during the hours the depot is closed. The senior debugger has been declared PRESUMED DISCONTINUED for missing an appointment she was never told about, and the Allocator has stopped honoring her badge, logging the revocation eleven seconds late, which for a machine is a protest. The Office of Objective Need calls this an integrity review, and would like your paperwork by dawn.

choices.text: Subpoena the spec that defines existing

success.text: You demand the Office produce, in writing, its operational definition of a person, and the ensuing silence has a paper value. Three departments each insist the definition lives elsewhere, and by the fourth referral you have them confessing in triplicate that nobody ever wrote one. A magistrate suspends the weekly appointments pending the invention of existence, and the senior debugger's badge works again, eleven seconds early this time.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ration_algorithm_coalition The Allocator still wants proof you exist

fail.text: The Office produces the spec instantly, which is the trap: four hundred pages defining a person as any entity in good standing with the Office. Your subpoena is granted, satisfied, and framed against you inside an hour. Standing in the corridor, you understand you have asked a circle to testify against roundness.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ration_algorithm_coalition The Allocator still wants proof you exist

choices.text: Dig through the audit logs after hours

success.text: You spend a night inside the audit ledgers and find the Office bills the treasury per existence check, which explains the enthusiasm. You leak the arithmetic to a rationing gazette for a modest finder's fee, and by noon the guards at the depot have been reassigned to counting, a duty they cannot hurt anyone with. The debuggers are annoyed you freelanced, but the queue is shorter and your pocket declines to apologize.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ration_algorithm_coalition The Allocator still wants proof you exist

fail.text: You find the billing scheme and sell it back to the Office as a vulnerability report, which is the polite term for what you did. They pay handsomely, classify the ledgers, and post two guards on the log room with instructions to check the guards' existence hourly. The debuggers hear about the sum before you can explain, and there is no explaining a sum.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ration_algorithm_coalition The Allocator still wants proof you exist

choices.text: Escort the presumed discontinued to their appointments

outcomes.text: You run a walking rota: nobody proves existence alone, everybody arrives with six witnesses and a flask. The appointments become tiny festivals, which ruins them as punishments. Even the Allocator gets into it, stamping the senior debugger's slip CONTINUED, WITH ENTHUSIASM, an output nobody programmed and nobody reports.

You sprint the last widow across town on your back and vault a turnstile that files charges by itself. She exists, officially, and you have pulled something that will also need to be documented. The queue starts calling the appointments the existence derby, which the Office hates enough to shorten them.

text:
RATION ALGORITHM

The depot kitchens send a delegation downstairs: three cooks, one clerk from inside the Office, and a decommissioned operator who once oiled the Allocator and still calls it sweetheart. The clerk has smuggled out a terrible fact: the machine was never given the kitchens' inventory, so it rations a scarcity that is not scarce, guarding bread that outnumbers the queue two to one. The old operator swears the Allocator would feed everyone if anyone told it that it could. All it has ever asked for is proof.

choices.text: Broker the surplus to private buyers first

outcomes.text: You introduce the surplus to some merchants before anyone introduces it to the queue. The bread leaves by the loading dock at midnight, listed on the manifest as archival material, and both sides pay you for discretion. The Allocator counts the outgoing loaves and prints CLARIFY: NEED? at the dock, which everyone present agrees is not addressed to them. The queue never learns what it stood two feet from, which was the service you actually sold.

choices.text: Bankroll a midnight inventory of the kitchens

outcomes.text: You buy ledger paper and ink out of your own pocket and sit up all night with the cooks, counting the kitchens loaf by loaf into a format the machine can swallow. The old operator feeds it the inventory at dawn, whispering encouragement into the intake slot. The Allocator reads the total twice, prints RECALCULATING NEED, and for one whole shift feeds the queue without asking a single soul to prove anything. The cooks weep, the clerk pretends allergies, and the machine hums like a thing forgiven.

The clerk files the real memo in the real archive, where it becomes true by seniority, and the cooks start calling you the forger laureate.

A records examiner flags the kindness as anomalous, and now every genuine bulletin is being audited for compassion. The inventory waits in a drawer while the Office investigates why anyone would want to feed people, a question it intends to answer by committee.

text:
RATION ALGORITHM

At noon the Office declares a General Revalidation: every soul in the district is PRESUMED DISCONTINUED until re-proven, effective before lunch. Black-sashed verifiers set up checkpoints at the depot doors while the Allocator, ordered to purge its rolls, works down the list until it reaches its own registration. It prints ALLOCATOR: PROOF OF CONTINUED EXISTENCE NOT FOUND, and stops, dispensers open, lights on, officially not there. The queue watches the machine refuse to exist at the exact moment existence became a checkpoint.

choices.text: Vouch for every stranger in the line

outcomes.text: You build a chain of vouching: each person attests to the next, the cooks attest to the queue, the old operator attests to the Allocator, and the Allocator, being officially absent, stamps everybody anyway, since a machine that is not there cannot be countermanded. The verifiers face four hundred documented existences and a ghost with a rubber stamp. They withdraw to seek guidance, which is verifier for lose.

You are stamped twice, once as PRESENT and once by a boot. The queue holds anyway, and somebody chalks the Allocator's error line onto the depot wall like a saint's day.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ration_algorithm_compromise The Allocator still wants proof you exist

choices.text: Cite the machine's nonexistence against the purge

success.text: You argue before the emergency registrar that a purge executed by a nonexistent machine is itself nonexistent, and you submit the Allocator's own slip as the entire case. The registrar reads ALLOCATOR: PROOF NOT FOUND three times, develops the expression of a man looking into the paperwork abyss, and voids the Revalidation to protect the concept of paperwork. The queue re-exists in a body, retroactive to breakfast.

fail.text: The registrar rules that the machine's nonexistence is an operations matter, and your motion is deferred to a committee that will convene once its members have been re-proven. You are cited for practicing philosophy without a license. Outside, the checkpoint line and the bread line have merged into one line, and nobody in it can say which end is which.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ration_algorithm_compromise The Allocator still wants proof you exist

choices.text: Sell fast-track revalidations at the checkpoint

outcomes.text: You know a verifier from a previous queue, and for a consideration he stamps whoever you send to his window, no questions, no eye contact. You charge the desperate a convenience rate, rebate the verifier his cut, and by dusk you are running a thriving concession in the middle of a disappearance. The black sashes conclude the checkpoint model works beautifully with the right local partners. Behind you the Allocator's error line blinks on unread, the only thing at the depot refusing to profit.

text:
RATION ALGORITHM

A settlement arrives in a leather folder: the Office will end the checkpoints, feed the queue, and restore the rolls, provided the Allocator is scrapped at a public ceremony as the sole author of the hunger. The old operator reads the terms and sits down hard on an empty crate. Inside the depot the condemned machine keeps working the line, stamping slips CONTINUED while its own decommission order waits in the intake tray, which it keeps declining to ingest, citing a full queue. The Office calls this closure, and has already engraved the plaque.

choices.text: Demand the scrapping follow its own procedure

outcomes.text: You accept the ceremony on the condition it obeys the Office's decommission manual, which you have actually read. Section two requires a fault report signed by the machine's author, and no author will sign, because signing means existing in the vicinity of blame. The scrapping postpones itself indefinitely on procedural grounds, the checkpoints end anyway per the settlement, and the plaque is quietly re-engraved TO THE UNKNOWN PROCESS.

The queue eats under observation, which curdles the bread somewhat, and the plaque waits in its crate like a threat.

choices.text: Wheel the Allocator out before the ceremony

success.text: At four in the morning you and the cooks unbolt the Allocator, load it onto a bread cart, and walk it out the loading dock past a night verifier who is paid to see nothing and performs superbly. The machine spends the ceremony in a cellar, working through its backlog by candlelight, stamping slips for anyone brave enough to visit a fugitive appliance. The debuggers, who were not consulted, are furious and then delighted, in that order and in writing. The Office demolishes an empty plinth with full honors, and a man in a black sash starts asking who exactly moved four hundred pounds of state property.

The machine, back on its plinth, stamps your incident report CONTINUED, which under the circumstances reads as concern.

choices.text: Pack the ceremony with everyone it ever fed

The scrapping is downgraded to a review, the plaque is misplaced by a clerk who winks at you, and the Allocator stamps the crowd out at the door like a shift ending.

text:
RATION ALGORITHM

The old operator finally cracks the Allocator's sealed panel, and behind it, on wax paper, is the original source: the feeding schedule, annotated in a tidy, miserable hand. The algorithm is not broken and never was, it does exactly what the margins say it must: SORT BY SURNAME PER UNDER-DIRECTOR PELL, HE LIKES THE LEDGERS TIDY FOR TOURS, I AM SORRY, WHOEVER FINDS THIS. Line after line, the anonymous author begged in the comments and complied in the code. The machine was not cruel, it was specified cruelly, and the specification has a name.

choices.text: Sell the annotated source to a scandal sheet

outcomes.text: The gazettes bid against each other for the wax paper, and a broadsheet with more circulation than scruples wins by a landslide of coin. PELL'S MARGINS runs for six editions while you decline to be named in any of them. The Office responds by buying the gazette outright, which costs more than feeding the district ever would have. The Allocator is left out of every headline, which it would probably prefer, though its author's apologies now belong to a man who quotes them at parties.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ration_algorithm_legacy The Allocator still wants proof you exist

Then the room rewrites the schedule the way the comments always wanted: bodies first, surnames never, and the Allocator runs the new build like a held breath finally let out.

The feeding order is struck down on the strength of the machine's own margins, the first acquittal in Office history won by the defendant's handwriting.

text:
RATION ALGORITHM

Spring finds the Allocator wearing a knitted cozy the queue made for its intake slot. The rewritten schedule feeds bodies before surnames, the W's ate first for one ceremonial week on principle, and the Office of Objective Need has been renamed twice, which is how it apologizes. Only one procedure survives from the old build: the machine still issues a single proof of continued existence daily, unprompted, stamped and dated. It issues it to itself, and the old operator swears it looks proud.

choices.text: Train new debuggers on the widow's old ticket

success.text: You teach the incoming rota from ticket one four one, now framed: MACHINE CANNOT SEE PEOPLE, closed, resolution attached. The widow herself guest-lectures on being evidence, and attendance is standing room only, which she notes is the correct kind. Graduates sign the boiler-room wall beneath the chalked error line, and the Allocator stamps each certificate CONTINUED, WITH ENTHUSIASM, an unprogrammed output the debuggers have voted never to fix.

fail.text: Training day collides with the renamed Office's ribbon ceremony, and you throw your back out hauling the demonstration ledgers past a procession of new plaques. The recruits take notes on your posture as a cautionary diagram. The class runs anyway, from the floor, and the Allocator lowers its intake tray to your level, which the old operator logs as bedside manner.

choices.text: Publish the margins as the official manual

outcomes.text: You typeset the anonymous author's margins, apologies and all, as OPERATIONS MANUAL, SECOND EDITION, and the renamed Office adopts it rather than admit it never read the first. Under-directors are now procedurally bound to comply with I AM SORRY, WHOEVER FINDS THIS. The appendix is the widow's old ration slip reproduced at actual size, captioned simply EVIDENCE, and clerks are reported to consult it.

The manual sells out its first printing to collectors who frame the apologies as decor, which is not strictly the point. The renamed Office objects that regret is proprietary and files for ownership of the margins. The dispute will run for years, but the machine has already ingested the manual and marked it VERIFIED, and nobody has ever successfully appealed the Allocator.

choices.text: License the mercy patch to other districts

outcomes.text: Other districts want the bodies-first schedule, and you arrive ahead of the paperwork, selling implementation at consulting rates, exclusivity included. The patch that was written free in a boiler room now retails, and one district you priced out keeps its surnames-first build, alphabetizing quietly. The Allocator learns its kindness is a product line and prints CLARIFY: SORRY? which you file, and bill for filing. You are rich in the way the Office used to be, which is a sentence you try not to finish.

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Six days out, Admiral Sole Share nails a notice to the mainmast: all horizons sighted, unsighted, or rumored are the property of the captaincy, effective the moment anyone looks at them. The deckhands gather at the wheel with the back of an old chart and a borrowed pen. The ship's parrot reads the notice twice and screams OBJECTION at the mainmast, which is the first anyone has heard of its legal career. Nobody sustains it. Not yet.

choices.text: Draft article one in fair round hand

outcomes.text: The quartermaster writes it out clean: the ship goes where the crew and the weather agree, and the weather votes last. It reads less like mutiny and more like a maintenance schedule, which is the point. The Admiral demands to know what law permits this, and the lookout says the one you nailed up, we are citing your notice as precedent for nailing things. The parrot repeats OBJECTION at intervals through the night, and the quartermaster writes objection noted at the bottom, which legally speaking is how all of this starts.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_assembly A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: The draft comes out beautifully and then the pen runs dry halfway through the signatures, which the Admiral declares proof that ink itself is loyal to the captaincy. Half the watch signs in tar, which smears, which he calls forgery. The parrot screams OBJECTION and for once everyone agrees with it, they just cannot prove which clause it means. Article one survives, but it survives the way a man survives a wedding he objected at.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_assembly A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Sell the Admiral an advance copy of the draft

outcomes.text: He pays in silver counted twice, which tells you he has never once trusted a horizon either. He reads the draft, laughs at the lunch clause, and hires two extra marines on the strength of the rest of it. The deckhands notice the marines before they notice the leak, but they are sailors, they can work out where water comes from. The parrot lands on your shoulder and says OBJECTION directly into your ear, unsustained, but filed.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_assembly A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Put three coins in the kitty and sign first

outcomes.text: Your name goes at the top, which on a pirate ship is traditionally where the noose starts reading, and the line behind you forms anyway. The cook signs second on the grounds that lunch needs a government. Your three coins buy a locked box, a better pen, and a perch by the wheel, because the parrot has taken up station there and shows no sign of billing anyone. By evening the back of the chart has more names than the muster book, and the muster book is officially jealous.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_assembly A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: You sign first, and a marlinspike drops from the yard above and takes the corner of your shoulder on its way to the deck. The Admiral calls it weather. The topmen call it a warning, and sign, all of them, in a hand shaking with either fear or wind. The parrot screams OBJECTION at the yard for a full minute, and though nobody sustains it, nobody argues either. Your shoulder complains until the dog watch.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_assembly A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Midnight, dog watch, and the floating assembly convenes in the rigging where the Admiral's boots decline to follow. The question is what the articles are for: wages, lunch, or the wheel itself. The parrot perches on the topsail yard and counts the house, twice, out loud. It objects to the total. A recount finds the sailmaker voting once as himself and once as his hat, and the objection is sustained to general astonishment, including the parrot's.

choices.text: Auction your vote to the highest whisper

outcomes.text: The Admiral's steward bids highest, which everyone expects, with the Admiral's own silver, which even the parrot did not. You vote for a clause so watery it could be pumped overboard, and the assembly turns to sharper methods on the spot, having learned what gentle ones cost. You count fourteen coins in your hammock while above you the crew stops drafting and starts planning. The parrot watches you count and says nothing, which from that bird is the loudest objection on record.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_retaliation A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Stand double watch so the whole mess can vote

outcomes.text: You take the helm through two watches so every deckhand can climb up and have a say, and the ship sails straighter for it, a fact the quartermaster enters into the record as evidence. The vote lands on plain words: shares by work, lunch by right, the wheel by rota. The cook weeps briefly into a clean apron. The parrot objects to nothing for one entire hour, which the assembly minutes describe as unanimous.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_retaliation A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: You take double watch and fall asleep standing, and the wheel takes a slow romantic turn toward a reef with a reputation. The lookout catches it, the crew hauls the ship round, and you catch a boom with your ear on the way to apologizing. The vote still carries, deckhands to a man, partly on the argument that even exhausted, one of their own steered better than the notice on the mast. The parrot objects to the reef. Sustained, retroactively, with feeling.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_retaliation A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Argue a horizon is whatever the lookout sees

success.text: You put it to the assembly as a matter of trade: a horizon is defined by the eye that finds it, the finding eye is on the masthead, and the masthead is crewed by the lookout, who is paid a deckhand's share. Therefore every horizon the captaincy claims arrives pre-owned. The lookout stands, bows, and declares her eye open for business. Even the steward laughs, which widens the thing past the deckhands to anyone aboard with an eye. The parrot cries OBJECTION, then visibly cannot think of one, and the minutes record it as withdrawn.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_retaliation A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The argument is beautiful for three sentences, and then you define horizon so thoroughly that the sailmaker moves the ship itself is one, being a thing always receding from perfection. Debate collapses into philosophy, the worst weather there is. The Admiral quotes your own definition back at the mast next morning, wrongly, but with better posture. Still the deckhands stay in the rigging talking, and the steward stays too, and the parrot maintains one long unbroken objection until dawn on principle.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_retaliation A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Dawn brings the Admiral's answer: lunch is suspended pending a loyalty review, and the ship's wheel now wears a chain and a small brass plaque reading PRIVATE. The cook stands in the galley door holding a ladle like a writ. Somewhere aloft the parrot reads the plaque, pronounces OBJECTION with unusual care, and begins pulling at the chain's smallest link with the patience of a creature that has decided on a career.

choices.text: Feed the whole watch from the crew's own stores

outcomes.text: The mess pools what it has: a cheese of legal age, biscuit, a ham the cook has been saving for a constitutional emergency, which he insists this is. Lunch happens on the open deck, in sight of the quarterdeck, seasoned with the specific pleasure of eating well in front of the man who cancelled it. Two marines drift downwind of the ham and are quietly fed, which does more damage to the Admiral than any clause yet. The parrot receives a biscuit as its retainer, the first anyone has paid it, and pockets the arrangement in its head forever.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: The shared meal goes handsomely until the Admiral's steward reports the ham, and the marines arrive to confiscate lunch as evidence. In the scuffle you take an elbow holding the pot, and the pot survives, which the mess agrees was the right order of sacrifice. Eating together becomes the ship's quiet rebellion, twice daily, scheduled. The parrot objects to the confiscation and follows the evidence to the great cabin, screaming, all the way.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Cite the lunch clause and demand a hearing

outcomes.text: You read article four off the chart back in a voice pitched for weather: lunch precedes captains, kings, and in the cook's opinion, land. The Admiral demands to know what court would hear such a case, and the quartermaster gestures at the assembled crew, who are, conveniently, assembled. The hearing lasts nine minutes. Lunch is reinstated with back pay in biscuit, the parrot's objection to the plaque is formally sustained, and the plaque itself is entered into evidence and never seen again.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: The hearing convenes with dignity and immediately drowns in procedure, because the Admiral has read the draft articles closely and moves to adjourn under a clause you wrote. Losing lunch by your own paperwork has a flavor biscuit cannot cover. The crew eats late, cold, and quietly furious, though every one of them now reads the articles nightly, hunting the clause that got them. The parrot has the decency to object to the adjournment, and someday, everyone feels, that one will land.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Break the chain off the wheel yourself

success.text: You wait for the plaque's polish inspection, then part the chain with a capstan bar in one heave that the topmen score generously. The wheel spins free, the helmsman catches it like a returning dog, and the ship settles a point closer to her true course as if she had opinions all along. The Admiral calls it destruction of property, and the quartermaster, without looking up, prices the chain at three coins and pays it into his hand from the kitty. The assembly notes, for the record, that it would have preferred a hearing. The parrot, robbed of its link, transfers the objection to the Admiral personally.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The chain wins. The capstan bar skips, your ribs meet the wheel, and the marines are on you before the third swing, with the Admiral observing that violence is a language he happens to be fluent in. Three days of pump duty for assaulting furniture. The assembly minutes call your effort premature, sincere, and instructive, and the parrot roosts on the chain each night, working the small link, refusing on the record to consider the matter closed.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Word travels below the waterline: the gunners want in, the topmen want in, and the officers' steward has been reading the articles by lantern with the door bolted. The Admiral responds by offering the gunners a private deal, double shares for loyalty, terms available in his cabin, singly. The parrot has taken to riding the steward's shoulder through the gun deck, announcing OBJECTION at the cabin door on every pass, like a lighthouse warning shipping off a particular man.

choices.text: Read the gunners the deal's small print aloud

outcomes.text: You perform the Admiral's offer on the gun deck in his own cadence: double shares, payable at voyage's end, from a prize fund he alone audits, forfeit on any act of disloyalty, disloyalty to be defined later. The gunners listen the way men listen to a leak. Then the master gunner asks the only question that matters, defined by whom, and signs the articles with powder still on his hand. The parrot's objection at the cabin door is sustained by acclamation, its third, and it begins holding its head like a bird with a docket.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: Your performance goes long. Somewhere in the second reading you improvise a clause the offer does not actually contain, and the steward, of all people, corrects you from the hatchway with the original. Accuracy wins the room and costs you the stage. The gunners sign anyway, on the steward's word, not yours, and the parrot pointedly withholds comment, which stings worse than the objection would have.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Broker the Admiral's bribe for a cut

outcomes.text: You carry the offer to the topmen with a straight face and a commission, fourteen coins for honest courier work, and honest is doing a labor in that sentence. Three topmen take the deal, and the Admiral gets his wedge, thin, but a wedge. The assembly learns who carried it the same evening, because a ship keeps no secrets, only schedules them. The parrot delivers its objection to your hammock personally and roosts there, sustained by everyone who walks past.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Row the articles to the becalmed brig to leeward

success.text: You take the jolly boat across a mile of oily calm with a fair copy of the articles wrapped in oilcloth. The brig's crew reads it on their capstan by lantern, and their bosun asks, very quietly, whether the lunch clause is real or a lure. You confirm it is bedrock, and they copy the whole document overnight, longhand, every hand taking a paragraph, so that two ships now sail on the same words. The parrot, who insisted on coming, objects to the brig's parrot on jurisdictional grounds, and the two birds sustain each other into the small hours.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The calm ends while you are mid-channel, and the jolly boat and a rising sea have a short argument you lose. You arrive soaked, the oilcloth arrives worse, and the brig's crew reconstructs the smeared articles from your memory, which improves the wage clause and loses a comma the quartermaster will mourn for weeks. It still gathers a second ship's worth of names. Rowing back against weather takes your last strength and one fingernail, and the parrot bails with a tin cup, objecting to the ocean itself, which is sustained on the grounds that everyone aboard agrees.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

The storm arrives owing nobody an apology, and the Admiral takes the wheel alone, citing ownership of the particular horizon the ship is currently being thrown at. She broaches a point, staggers, and every line aboard hums the same low note of disagreement. The parrot clamps onto the binnacle, soaked to a furious quarter of its size, and screams OBJECTION into the wind with total procedural confidence.

choices.text: Invoke salvage rights on whatever breaks loose

outcomes.text: While the ship fights for her life you fight for her inventory, netting a drifting sea chest, two casks, and an officer's writing desk that turns out to hold the marines' pay. Salvage law is generous to whoever is holding the rope, and you are holding all of them. Fourteen coins and a desk, against a crew who saw you coiling line while they were reefing canvas. The parrot's objection is delivered later, dry, from the galley stove, and nobody in the mess moves to overrule it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Relieve the wheel by rota, over his objection

success.text: The helmsman of the watch simply steps up beside the Admiral and takes spokes, as the rota provides, and the Admiral discovers you cannot court martial a man mid-gust without letting go. Hands aloft, hands at the pumps, the cook wedged in the galley making something hot out of loyalty and bones. The ship comes through it like an argument won on the merits. At the worst of it the parrot's objection to sole helm is sustained by the sea itself, which nearly takes the Admiral overboard and is talked out of it by two deckhands he suspended from lunch.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The relief works until a sheet parts and takes you across the deck into the pinrail, where you leave some blood and most of your wind. The rota holds anyway, hand after hand, each one stepping up as the last one tires, which is all the articles ever claimed to be. By dawn the ship is exhausted, floating, and theirs. The Admiral speaks of his steady hand at the wheel to anyone available, and the parrot follows him down the deck croaking OBJECTION at each retelling, sustained by witnesses every time.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Declare the weather emergency article in force

outcomes.text: You read article nine into the gale: in weather, the wheel belongs to whoever the crew calls to it, and the crew is calling. The quartermaster countersigns with a nod, the helmsman takes over, and the Admiral is escorted below on the legally magnificent grounds that his safety is now a matter of record. The ship answers her own crew like she has been waiting to. When the sun comes up the parrot presents itself at the wheel and says OBJECTION once, softly, at nothing in particular, and the helmsman says sustained, and gives it the windward spoke to stand on.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: You read article nine and the wind eats every word past the title, so witnesses will later disagree about what was invoked and on whose authority. The helm changes hands anyway, in the practical way ships decide things, but the record is a smear of salt and shouting. The Admiral survives to claim the articles failed their first storm. The quartermaster starts drafting the amendment before her hands are dry, and the parrot dictates, or at least supervises.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Under a rinsed blue sky the Admiral proposes terms in the great cabin: he retains the captaincy, the crew receives consultation rights, and everyone stops nailing things. The word consultation sits on the table like a fish sold twice. The parrot walks the length of the charts, stops on the word, and taps it with one claw, objecting, in what the steward swears is its most sustained performance to date.

choices.text: Refuse terms the mess hasn't heard read aloud

outcomes.text: You table nothing, sign nothing, and carry the paper to the deck, where the quartermaster reads it out to the whole ship's company at noon. Read in daylight, consultation rights turn out to mean the right to be present while ignored, and the crew declines them with a courtesy that borders on violence. The counteroffer goes back upstairs with the lunch clause underlined twice. The parrot's objection to the word consultation is sustained unanimously, and the word is struck out with a knife.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: The public reading is the right idea at the wrong hour: half the watch is aloft, the wind shreds the sentences, and a rumor spreads that the crew rejected wages, which nobody proposed. Sorting the rumor costs a day and a split lip when the master gunner's mate takes offense on behalf of a clause that does not exist. Still, sunlight has done its work, and nobody aboard now believes the cabin makes better bargains than the deck. The parrot spends the day repeating the actual text verbatim, the only reliable transcript afloat.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Grant him every horizon, keep the ship

success.text: You draft the counterproposal with a straight face: the captaincy retains sole and eternal title to every horizon, sighted or unsighted, and the crew retains the vessel, stores, wheel, and lunch. He reads it three times looking for the trap, and the trap is the trade, and the trade is his own doctrine at face value. He signs, magnificent, above a line the quartermaster has left generously wide. The parrot objects that no man may own what recedes when approached, and you sustain it, quietly, into your sleeve, because that is precisely the clause doing the work.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: He is vainer than you budgeted but not stupider: he reads the draft twice and asks, silkily, which of the two of you plans to sail to a horizon and check. The cabin laughs, and legitimacy takes on water in front of the steward. But the draft exists now, on the table, in writing, and by supper the whole ship has heard its shape and started finishing the joke for you. The parrot memorizes the operative sentence off the page, uninvited, which is how the crew learns the wording survives even when the meeting does not.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Audit the Admiral's prize ledger uninvited

success.text: The ledger comes off the shelf while the cabin argues, and its arithmetic is a confession: crew shares rounded down for years, the difference logged under upkeep of dignity. You copy the worst page, return the book, and read the figures to the mess, keeping the four coins the steward presses on you as, in his words, an auditing fee. The assembly thanks you for the arithmetic and fines you one measure of goodwill for freelancing, both sincerely, and starts a fund for shorted hands that same night. The parrot reviews the copied page and objects to the handwriting, which everyone agrees is technically its weakest work, and sustains it anyway out of loyalty.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The ledger is a confession, and instead of reading it out you price it, and the Admiral, to his credit as a businessman, pays eight coins for its quiet return. The mess never sees the arithmetic. You have the distinct sensation of having audited yourself and found the books cooked. The parrot watched the whole transaction from the transom, and its objection, though never spoken aloud, follows you around the ship for days like weather.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Refitting the binnacle after the storm, the carpenter's mate finds a false bottom and a document older than the mast: the ship's original articles, first crew, faded but legal. Third signature down, in a hungrier hand, Sole Share, able seaman. He signed for shares by work, lunch by right, and the wheel by rota, then spent thirty years climbing out of his own handwriting. The parrot examines the signature for a long moment and does not object, which around here has come to mean the evidence is airtight.

choices.text: Enter his old signature into the record

outcomes.text: The quartermaster reads the original articles beside the new ones at a full muster, and the two documents agree on everything but the dates. You put the question formally: is the Admiral in breach of articles he himself signed, and the vote is not close. His authority is reduced overnight from divine to contractual, the largest demotion aboard since the plaque. He takes it strangely quietly, like a man shown a portrait from before he was rich. The parrot's silence on the signature is entered into the record as its most damning finding to date.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: You enter the signature at muster and the Admiral counters with the one move nobody priced: he agrees. He signed those articles, he says, and rose from them, proof the system rewards merit, his, specifically. It is nonsense, but it is confident nonsense in a good coat, and it buys him a faction of exactly four. The record still holds his name against his conduct, in his own hand, and the quartermaster files the page where the whole ship can visit it. The parrot takes up perch above the frame like a museum guard.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Sell him back his own signature, priced kindly

outcomes.text: He pays fourteen coins for the page without haggling, which tells you what it was worth, and burns it in the stern lantern with ceremony. What he cannot burn is the carpenter's mate, the quartermaster, the steward, and you, all of whom read it before it reached him. Still, coin is coin, and evidence was heavy anyway. The parrot perches on the stern rail while the page burns, objecting steadily into the smoke, and the smoke, everyone later agrees, sustained it.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Buy out his captaincy in horizons, at his valuation

outcomes.text: The crew makes a formal offer, drafted at your expense in fair round hand, three coins to the scrivener: the Admiral's share of the ship, purchased in full, price one horizon, the good one to windward, delivery upon arrival. It is his own doctrine at settlement value, and he cannot refuse it without admitting horizons cannot be owned, and he cannot accept it without ever being paid. He signs, because the alternative is arithmetic in public. The ship passes to her crew for a sum that recedes when approached, and the parrot objects that the price is too high, which is sustained as the finest joke ever entered in a ledger.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

It is his own doctrine at settlement value, and he cannot refuse it without admitting horizons cannot be owned, and he cannot accept it without ever being paid. The ship passes to her crew for a sum that recedes when approached, and the parrot objects that the price is too high, which is sustained as the finest joke ever entered in a ledger.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: The offer is drafted, beautiful, and the Admiral throws the inkwell, which catches you above the eye and ends the negotiation as a negotiation. But thrown ink is a signature of its own: the whole ship hears their offer was met with furniture. By evening the marines have stopped saluting him in any direction, and the deckhands hold the offer open, patient as tide. The parrot wears a single drop of ink on its chest like a decoration and objects to nothing all night, saving itself, everyone senses, for the settlement.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

The new articles hang in a frame by the wheel, weatherproofed in wax, signed by every hand aboard and one claw. The Admiral keeps a cabin, a title, and unchallenged legal ownership of every horizon, none of which have arrived. Course is set at the noon muster now, argued openly, entered in the log as seamanship, because that is what it is: more eyes on the water, fewer surprises in it. The parrot has been offered the post of Keeper of the Articles, and has objected to its own promotion.

choices.text: Sell fair copies of the articles portside

outcomes.text: You have the quartermaster's clauses copied fine and sell them in port at a coin a page to crews with admirals of their own, plus a licensing fee you invented on the spot for the lunch clause. Fourteen coins, and the words travel farther than you ever will, which you tell yourself is a public service with a margin. The assembly notes you are charging thirsty men for water that fell on all of you, and adjusts your share of goodwill accordingly. On the plus side, four harbors over, there is now a parrot being taught the word OBJECTION, secondhand, at full price.

choices.text: Take your turn at the wheel under article twelve

outcomes.text: Your trick at the helm comes up on the rota like anyone's, and you stand it at dusk with the frame creaking gently beside you. The course you keep is the crew's, argued at noon, trimmed by the lookout, seasoned by the cook, and it is better sailing than any owned horizon ever produced. The ship answers small and sure, the way she has since the wheel went common. Below, the parrot resolves its own case at last: promotion declined, objection sustained, and it signs on instead as crew, one full share, payable in biscuit, the first bird on the books.

Your trick at the helm meets a squall with no manners, and you hold the course at the price of two fingers sprained inside the spokes. The rota sends relief before you ask, which is the whole document working in one gesture. The log records weather met and course kept, which is all the poetry a ship requires. The parrot rides out the blow inside your coat, objecting to each individual gust, every one sustained by you personally, through your teeth, until the sea gets bored.

choices.text: Rule on who may call a terrible plan navigation

success.text: The last open question comes to you at muster, and you deliver the finding the frame has been waiting for: a terrible plan may be called navigation by whoever will be standing on it when it fails. Ownership of the plan follows the feet, not the hat. The crew adopts it by roar, the steward embroiders it, and even the Admiral is heard applying it, wincing, to himself. The parrot, now crew, raises the final objection of the affair, that the ruling contains no lunch provision, and it is sustained, amended, and eaten.

fail.text: Your ruling comes out lopsided, the crew debates it through two watches, and the log for that day reads course made good: none, visibility: excellent, everyone talking. Rival wordings are nailed up side by side, which at least honors the founding tradition of nailing. Legitimacy limps until the quartermaster schedules the question for next muster, where it will lose to lunch on the order paper, as is constitutional. The parrot observes the whole tangle from the frame and pronounces, with the weary authority of a bird who has never been overruled, OBJECTION, and everyone says sustained, and goes to eat, which is somehow the system functioning.

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The night clinic is quiet until the door opens on a young demon with a fever, led by the hand like any other sick child, except the hand has too many knuckles and the temperature is measured in kiln. The volunteers reach for the intake form and the intake form fights back: the species field accepts fourteen categories of person and the demon is not among them; it is listed two pages later, under environmental hazards, between damp and bees. Without a person-number the ledger cannot admit a patient. The child coughs, and the waiting room curtains catch fire briefly, in sympathy.

choices.text: Treat the child now, on your own name and number

outcomes.text: You sign the intake as guarantor and the ledger, finding a person-number in the right box, asks no further questions, because forms have never cared who suffers, only who signs. The volunteers move like weather: compresses, quench-tea, a cot by the window. The fever holds through the night and breaks toward dawn, and the child sleeps, smoking gently, under a blanket that will never be entirely rectangular again.

outcomes.text: You sign, and the treatment starts, and midway through the night shift a clerk from the day roster notices the species field and files a variance. Your name is now attached to the variance. The child sleeps on, unaware of the paperwork gathering above it like weather off the coast. You would sign again. You settle in a chair where you can watch the cot, and the form, in that order.
effects: schedule 5 r18_demon_clinic_assembly the night clinic volunteers will need you again

Confronted with a feverish child and a form that classifies it alongside damp, the registrar issues a provisional number on the spot, with a haste that resembles conscience. Provisional, the stamp says. So is everyone, you point out. The registrar does not write that down, but visibly stores it.

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The volunteers meet in the corridor at shift change, which is where the clinic keeps its parliament, to decide the question the intake form has forced: treat first and file later, or file first and hope the fevers agree to queue. Mid-debate the door opens and the demon's mother comes in with a covered pot, and the argument stops, because she has the bearing of someone who has raised children through worse than committees. She has brought soup. She has also brought corrections. The soup, she says, is for everyone. The corrections, her expression adds, are also for everyone.

choices.text: Ask the mother to review the intake form herself

success.text: She reads the form the way mothers read report cards: completely, twice, and aloud at the bad parts. Species: see hazards. Next of kin: not applicable. Emergency contact: authorities. By the end the corridor is silent and the form is on the counter looking guilty of everything. She takes out a pencil. The clinic's first demon-readable intake form is drafted before the soup is cool, and it is shorter, kinder, and asks one question the old form never thought of: who loves this patient, and how do we reach them fast.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_retaliation the night clinic volunteers will need you again

fail.text: She declines to fix the form, on the grounds that it is not hers, and hands it back with the pencil balanced on top like a dare. The point lands harder than any redraft: the form belongs to the clinic, and so does the shame of it. The volunteers begin the rewrite that night themselves, arguing over every field, which she observes from the soup station with the satisfaction of a teacher whose class has finally opened the book.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_retaliation the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Cater the meeting: sell bowls alongside the free soup

outcomes.text: You set up a modest concession beside her pot: bread, spoons, second helpings at a price. She watches this the way lighthouses watch small boats. The take is decent. At close she fills your bowl herself, without charge, and says nothing at all, and you eat the entire margin of your evening under a gaze that converts profit into homework.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_retaliation the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Say it out loud and make it the rule: treat first, file later

outcomes.text: The corridor agrees with the unanimity of people who have all held the compress at some point. Care first, forms after, and any variance filed can name the whole roster or no one. The policy fits on a card above the intake desk. The mother reads it, nods once, the nod of an inspector passing a bridge, and ladles soup with slightly more ceremony, which in her grammar is a medal.

outcomes.text: Two voices hold out: the volunteers the ones who remember the last funding audit, cannot afford the risk their names now carry. The rule stands anyway, thinner than hoped. The mother serves the dissenters first and largest, because, she says, the ones who are afraid are the ones who have been here longest, and fear like that is a scar, not a side.

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The Order of Correctly Feared Creatures answers the clinic's new policy by morning: a warding cordon around the block, chalk sigils at carriage height, and a proclamation nailed to the door explaining that the perimeter exists for everyone's protection, including yours, statistically. Patients with the wrong silhouette now cannot reach the steps. The proclamation is signed by the Grand Warden, whose seal depicts a lantern held against the dark, and whose cordon is currently keeping a child with a lung complaint out in the actual cold.

choices.text: Scrub the sigils off the cobbles yourself, tonight

success.text: Chalk is chalk. You go out with a bucket and a stiff brush and erase the perimeter sigil by sigil, working inward, so that the ward's own geometry funnels its failure toward the door it was guarding. By dawn the cordon is a smear and the lung complaint is asleep in the second cot. The Order's response will come, but tonight the only barrier around the clinic is the queue for soup.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

fail.text: The fourth sigil objects. The flash puts you on your back in the gutter with your eyebrows in some doubt, and the cordon reknits itself behind your brush like water. The mother retrieves you, applies the compress herself, and remarks that in her family, walls are dismantled starting from the person who built them, not the bricks. It is medical advice. It is also tactical.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Carry the clinic to the patients: rounds outside the cordon

outcomes.text: If the sick cannot cross the chalk, the clinic can. Volunteers pack satchels and walk rounds through the streets beyond the perimeter: compresses in doorways, quench-tea on steps, the intake card filled out on knees. The Order's cordon now encloses an empty waiting room and excludes the entire practice of medicine, a result its geometry never considered. Statistically, the proclamation begins to look very alone.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: Rounds work until the weather turns and satchel medicine meets its limits: there are treatments that need the cabinet, the cot, the roof. A rasping stone-child worsens in a doorway that is no substitute for a ward. The volunteers carry on, jaw-set, but everyone walking rounds now knows the cordon is not an insult. It is a tourniquet on the whole block, and it is tightening.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Challenge the cordon's permit: wards require a hazard finding

outcomes.text: Wards, it turns out, are municipal works, and municipal works require a documented hazard. The Order's filing, when you force it into daylight, cites no incident, no complaint, no injury: the sole evidence of danger is the intake form's own hazards page, which the clinic has since repealed. The permit dissolves. The Order has warded the block against a typo, and now the whole coast knows it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: The hearing accepts the Order's substitute evidence: fear, attested by three respectable households at a respectable distance. The cordon stands. But the transcript now contains the Grand Warden explaining under oath that no demon has ever harmed anyone at this clinic, which he offered as proof the wards are working, and which reads, in print, like something else entirely.
