choices.text: Move half a ton of bender in the four minutes

success.text: Eight of you take the weight in silence, walking the bender across the floor in a rehearsed shuffle timed to the whistle, and the day shift arrives to find furniture where a weapon jig stood. The Toolsetter calls it the finest shift change since the flood year and buys the tea. Gerald gets promoted to doorstop for the tooling cage, his first job with responsibilities.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_retaliation The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The bender comes down on the last yard and finds your foot under it, and the clang travels three streets, which at four in the morning is a form of publicity. A dockhand asks next day what the shell works drops at night, and the answer going round is anvils, which nobody believes. Your foot turns an honest purple, and the Toolsetter enters it in the true ledger as plant damage, sentimental.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_retaliation The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

A junior auditor arrives with a clipboard, a laminated authority, and a face that has never needed shaving. Peace Through Quarterly Growth wants every kinetic deliverable counted by dawn, and the shift has taken to calling him the Compliance Cherub before he reaches the door. Gerald stands primed and sheeted among forty crates, wearing a stencil that says PUMP, MISC. The count starts at midnight, which is your territory, not his.

choices.text: Read the Cherub's count sheet before he can

success.text: While the Cherub warms his hands, you read his master sheet upside down and learn something interesting: head office lists this factory's entire output as durable goods, miscellaneous. You correct two of his arithmetic errors, collect the audit assistance stipend for cooperation, and file the phrase away where you keep loaded things. The crew notes how fluently you read company paper and decides to keep half an eye on you, which is fair.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: You sell the Cherub a reconciled count that flatters head office and yourself, and he pays the discrepancy bounty out of a petty cash tin the size of a coffin. The reconciliation shaves the night shift's logged hours to make the totals sing, and somebody's overtime quietly dies for it. The envelope is heavy, and the canteen gets quieter when you enter it, and those two facts are related.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Run the pallet shuffle while he counts

outcomes.text: The same forty crates pass the Cherub four times in four positions, moved between aisles by a crew that has rehearsed boredom to a professional standard. He counts one hundred sixty deliverables, which is the exact number on his sheet, and goes home proud. Gerald crosses the floor twice in the rotation, sheeted, counted both times as PUMP, MISC, and thereby earns his first two official existences.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: A pallet corner takes you across the ribs on the third rotation, and your gasp is loud enough that the Cherub looks up and starts a fresh page. He finds nothing, because there is nothing findable, but he writes ATMOSPHERE: EVASIVE in his notes and underlines it. The district hears the shell works got audited at midnight, and every retelling adds a zero.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Demand his Form 77-K before a single crate opens

success.text: You inform the Cherub that enumeration without a Form 77-K, Authority to Count, is itself a countable offense, and watch him search a briefcase that contains only a 77-J. The audit adjourns until head office can post the correct form, which gives the line nine days and the paint locker a holiday. Gerald spends them under his sheet, legally uncountable, the happiest he has ever been.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The Cherub produces the 77-K, laminated, signed in three inks, and thanks you for your diligence in a voice that files things. Your objection goes into his notes as obstruction, adjacent, and the count proceeds with him watching you specifically. It could be worse, and the beautiful ledger will need to be more beautiful.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

The day shift eats lunch in the same canteen, and their senior fitter has noticed that station two now bends hinges. She sits down opposite you, sets her sandwich aside, and says her brother's town took a delivery of this factory's produce two winters back. She does not say it like a joke, and nobody at the table treats it like one. She wants in, and she wants to know the rota.

choices.text: Cut the day shift in and split the tooling bill

outcomes.text: The kitty opens, three coins lighter, and the senior fitter's crew takes the daylight half of the work: two shifts, one secret, twice the pace. Station three becomes a stove press by Friday, and the canteen starts smelling like an actual future. Gerald is moved beneath the serving hatch, sheeted, where the day shift leaves him a biscuit on principle.
effects: schedule 3 r18_arms_factory_crisis The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

The crash brings half the street to the gate asking what the shell works drops in daylight now, and the answer, anvils again, has stopped working. The senior fitter hauls you up, checks your arms, and says the day shift breaks bigger things than that before lunch.
effects: schedule 3 r18_arms_factory_crisis The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Drill everyone in fluent quarterly vocabulary

outcomes.text: By Thursday the whole floor can say throughput-positive warmth vertical without laughing, and visiting managers hear their own language spoken back at native speed and retreat reassured. A stove is now a customer-facing heat solution, a pump is decentralized hydration, and the fuse line's absence is a legacy divestment. Gerald's stencil is upgraded to LEGACY ASSET, and legacy assets, per policy, are nobody's job.

By Friday somebody has chalked an arrow on Gerald's sheet pointing at station five, and nobody admits to the handwriting.

text:
ARMS FACTORY

Head office descends on a Tuesday, eleven coats that have never been near machine oil, led by the Vice President of Outcomes. Peace Through Quarterly Growth has noticed that its mourning output is down and its hinge revenue is up, and it would like the numbers to explain themselves. Worse, dispatch has followed the beautiful ledger to its logical end and staged Gerald on the outbound dock as AGRICULTURAL BOILER, SINGLE USE. The truck comes at six.

choices.text: Walk the VP through the beautiful ledger yourself

success.text: You take the Vice President of Outcomes line by line through a ledger tidier than his own company, where every pump descends from an order and every order from a clause with a pedigree. He finds one entry he does not like, boiler, diplomatic, single use, and you explain it is a treaty gift, which is, historically, true. He initials the page, and Gerald becomes the only bomb ever vouched for by the man sent to find him.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_compromise The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: Page forty sticks to page forty one, and the Vice President of Outcomes reads two totals at once that should never have met. You argue the ledger's case for an hour and win on points, but he leaves with a photograph of page forty and the word discrepancy in his mouth. On the dock, Gerald waits for the six o'clock truck like a package with somewhere to be.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_compromise The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Sell the VP a glowing report on his own visit

outcomes.text: You draft the Vice President of Outcomes a site assessment praising his decisive leadership of a turnaround he has not understood, and he pays for it out of a budget line called insight. He presents it upstairs, upstairs raises the kinetic quota at some other factory to match his success, and the envelope in your coat weighs about the same as that sentence. Gerald stays on the dock, boiler, single use, next to a crate of actual boilers who are learning terrible habits from him.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_compromise The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Flood the floor with immaculate boring work

outcomes.text: Every station performs perfection at half speed, a factory impersonating its own training film, and eleven coats watch pumps being born until their eyes water with reassurance. Behind the tour, two fitters wheel Gerald off the dock and into the tooling cage under a manifest that says RETURNED, INSUFFICIENT DIPLOMACY. The Vice President of Outcomes leaves impressed and none the wiser, which for him is one condition, not two.

The Vice President of Outcomes watches you bleed on a stove and notes that safety culture is an area of opportunity. On the dock, Gerald's truck is delayed, not canceled, and the district talks for days about the morning the coats came.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_compromise The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

The Vice President of Outcomes offers terms in the loading bay while the six o'clock truck idles at the gate with Gerald lashed aboard. Half the line returns to kinetic deliverables, nobody asks how the other half got converted, and everyone receives a bonus called flexibility recognition. He calls this generous, and by his arithmetic it is. The truck's manifest still says AGRICULTURAL BOILER, SINGLE USE, and the driver is finishing a sandwich.

choices.text: Swing the gantry crane across the gate, slowly

success.text: You walk the overhead crane down the bay at ceremonial speed and set a crate of stoves in the truck's path with the tenderness of a state funeral. The driver looks at the crate, looks at his watch, and clocks off, because moving it is a different job classification. Gerald comes off the truck in the confusion, and the Vice President of Outcomes learns that the factory's machinery has opinions. The crew is grateful and rattled in equal parts, because cranes are not meant to editorialize.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The crane load swings wide, and you take the corner of a stove crate across the shoulder hard enough to change your relationship with that shoulder. The truck leaves in the confusion, and you chase it on a borrowed bicycle for two miles until the border checkpoint bounces it back, single use boilers lacking an import class. Gerald returns to the factory by teatime, unshipped and smug, while you return by cart, at an angle. The VP logs the incident as equipment enthusiasm and reopens the kinetic question.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Decline the bonus together, politely, in writing

outcomes.text: The whole shift signs one page declining flexibility recognition on the grounds that the flexibility is already theirs and they are not selling it back. The Vice President of Outcomes has spreadsheets for strikes and spreadsheets for demands, and nothing at all for a polite no with forty signatures. He withdraws the terms to seek guidance, and the truck is unloaded pending guidance, which never comes. Gerald spends the evening back in the tooling cage, being leaned on affectionately.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: The VP answers the letter with a mandatory realignment meeting in the unheated bay, two hours standing, agenda: gratitude. You come out of it with a cough that stays a fortnight and a district full of talk that the shell works is refusing money now, which sounds mad even to the sympathetic. The letter worked, in that the terms are dead, but so is everyone's evening.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Accept his terms, then define every word in them

outcomes.text: You accept in full, subject to definitions, and define kinetic deliverable as any product with moving parts, which pumps have, and hinges are, and stoves contain. The VP signs before reading the appendix, because signing before reading is how his career was built. Half the line is now contractually obligated to make pumps, and the other half, per the same clause, may continue making pumps. Gerald is exempted by name as a diplomatic instrument, which he will not let the other inventory forget.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: The VP's traveling lawyer counter-defines kinetic with a citation from the original shell contract, and suddenly you are on record accepting terms that mean whatever he says they mean. The deal stalls in definitions committee, which buys the line three weeks but costs you the appearance of cleverness. The truck stays in the yard, undriven, while both sides argue about what a boiler is.

text:
ARMS FACTORY

Deep in the company archive, the Compliance Cherub has found the thing nobody was supposed to find, and he brings it to the night shift at two in the morning, shaking. For nine years, for tariff and insurance reasons, Peace Through Quarterly Growth has booked every shell it ever shipped as durable goods, agricultural. There have never, officially, been any bombs. The audit cannot prove the plowshares were ever anything else, because the company got there first, in writing, for money.

choices.text: Pour him tea and show him the true ledger

success.text: You sit the Cherub down in the break room and open the true ledger, the one with real numbers and, in the back, the Toolsetter's list of names from shipment 41, kept in his cap for nine years and copied here so the cap is not the only place. The Cherub reads for a long time, and nobody hurries him. At dawn he signs the audit closed in his own hand, asks to keep his clipboard, and asks, quieter, whether the day shift is hiring. Gerald, present as always, holds the biscuits.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The Cherub takes the true ledger badly at first, and talking him through it costs the whole night and most of your voice, and at one point he tries to resign to you personally, which is not a thing. By morning he is steady, but a watchman saw the break room lit till dawn, and the district now believes the shell works holds seances. You sleep standing up against the hinge bender, which leaves a mark. It was worth it, probably, the way true things are.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Certify continuity with the company's own archive

outcomes.text: You file a continuity certification stating that the factory continues its documented historical product line, durable goods, agricultural, and attach nine years of the company's own manifests as evidence. It is unanswerable, because answering it would require Peace Through Quarterly Growth to declare in writing that it has been arming a continent under a farm tariff. The company chooses the pumps. Gerald is reclassified, per his stamp, as diplomacy handling equipment, and the filing clerk does not blink, because filing clerks have seen things.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: Your certification runs to forty pages, which is thirty nine more than boring, and an archivist upstairs requests definitions of the word continuity. The request means nothing and can be answered with more paper, but the folder with the factory's name is now thick, and thick folders attract readers. The line holds its breath and keeps making stoves.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Sell the tariff secret to the quiet broker

outcomes.text: The broker pays a year's wages for the shape of the euphemism, because knowing how Peace Through Quarterly Growth books its shipping is worth exactly that to men who move cargo past tariffs. You take the envelopes, and somewhere down the coast a scheme you will never see begins pricing itself accordingly. The Cherub watches you take the money with an expression you last saw in a mirror. Gerald's sheet slips off that night, and nobody rushes to cover him.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

Six months on, the factory smells of solder and soup instead of cordite, and the order board lists pumps, hinges, and stoves in a font nobody had to invent a euphemism for. The audit concluded, in writing, that the factory has always made what it now makes, and Peace Through Quarterly Growth reports the pivot as its own strategic vision. In the break room, defused over one careful weekend by the Toolsetter, Gerald stands holding the kettle, stamp intact: HANDLE WITH DIPLOMACY. The shift does, every morning.

choices.text: Author the quarterly report head office deserves

outcomes.text: You write Peace Through Quarterly Growth a quarterly of unbearable beauty: legacy divestment complete, warmth verticals at capacity, mourning down across all markets and nobody asking why. Head office frames it, hands out bonuses upstairs, and reduces the factory's oversight tier to minimal, on the grounds that success requires no supervision. Gerald appears in the appendix as a long-term diplomatic asset, depreciating gracefully. The kettle boils on schedule.

You write Peace Through Quarterly Growth a quarterly of unbearable beauty: legacy divestment complete, warmth verticals at capacity, mourning down across all markets and nobody asking why. Head office frames it, hands out bonuses upstairs, and reduces the factory's oversight tier to minimal, on the grounds that success requires no supervision. Gerald appears in the appendix as a long-term diplomatic asset, depreciating gracefully. The kettle boils on schedule.

Your report is so persuasive that head office announces a best-practices delegation to study the miracle factory, arrival Thursday. The line spends three days teaching stoves to look like strategy, and the strain shows in the tea. The delegation leaves converted, in the commercial sense, but the district has now seen two visitations in a season and draws conclusions at volume.

choices.text: Charge a tour fee to see the line the audit blessed

outcomes.text: Word has spread of the factory the audit could not decode, and manufacturers arrive weekly to learn the trick, so you charge at the door and narrate freely, omitting only everything that matters. The fees are handsome and the tours are useless, which is the correct ratio. Gerald is the final stop, kettle steaming on his shoulders, and visitors photograph his stamp believing it is a joke, which suits everyone in the room except possibly Gerald.

Gerald is the final stop, kettle steaming on his shoulders, and visitors photograph his stamp believing it is a joke, which suits everyone in the room except possibly Gerald.

choices.text: Take the night wrench full time and train the next shift

success.text: The Toolsetter hands over the night wrench with both hands, the way you pass a thing that has been used for something that mattered. You take on three apprentices, teach them tolerances first and vocabulary second, and add a line to the rota that simply says whoever holds the wrench answers for the line. On quiet mornings the shift takes its tea standing near Gerald, not for any reason anyone states. The kettle sees to the rest.

fail.text: Your first week of training, the hinge bender takes a demonstration too literally and leaves you a scar shaped roughly like a hinge, which the apprentices find funnier than you do. The clatter of your teaching carries, and the district decides the shell works is arming again, and has to be talked down street by street. The rota gets your name on it anyway, in the Toolsetter's hand. Scars, he says, are just tenure.

text:
MINE RESCUE

At six minutes past the whistle, the north shaft of the Bottomless Extraction pit sits down like a tired animal, taking the whole second shift with it. Eleven names stay chalked on the tally board, and the tally-keeper puts her thumb over the rag so nobody wipes them. Head office responds within the hour, which impresses everyone until the response is read aloud: rescue operations may commence upon completion of Form RA-77, Risk Assessment of Proposed Rescue, in triplicate. The regional manager, a canary named Gareth, is brought to the pithead in his executive cage to observe.

choices.text: File the emergency exemption before the ink dries

outcomes.text: You cite subsection nine, the one about imminent loss of company property, and list the crew as company property with a straight face. The clerk stamps it because the wording is technically beautiful. Digging is authorized pending review, and head office, wrong-footed, suspends pit-cottage rents for the duration to look humane in the record. The tally-keeper lets you touch the board, once.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: Your exemption is received, numbered, and placed in the queue behind a dispute about ladder width. The clerk is sympathetic in the way of a man reading from a card that says be sympathetic. Above ground the crowd gets louder, and below ground the tapping stays exactly as patient as before, which is somehow worse.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Take the company coin to keep the crowd orderly

outcomes.text: Bottomless Extraction hires you on the spot as Community Calm Facilitator, eight now, six when the crowd goes home. You walk the rope line saying the word procedure in a soothing voice, and it works, which is the upsetting part. Gareth watches you from his cage with the expression of one professional recognizing another.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Grab a shovel and sign the rescue rota

outcomes.text: You buy your own gloves, which turns out to be the entry fee, and the tally-keeper writes you onto the rota under volunteers, no pay, all weather. By dark you know eleven names and what each man tapped last. The pit-cottage landlords quietly stop collecting for the week, sensing the weather. Somewhere behind you a clerk records the digging as unauthorized soil relocation, and nobody stops to correct him.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The first rock you pull frees three more with opinions. You leave a knuckle in the rubble and gain a bandage from the widow who runs the tea urn, which formalizes your membership better than any card. The crowd sees the blood and gets loud enough that head office logs a disturbance.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

Day two opens with a flip chart. The deputy assessor of foreseeable events, sent up from head office with a laminated agenda, convenes an information session titled Managing Expectations Around the North Shaft Event. He would like to be liked, and has brought pastries. Gareth attends in his capacity as regional manager and does not sing, which the miners' wives notice, because a canary that stops singing is telling you something. The tapping from below has settled into a rhythm the old men translate as: still here, send soup.

choices.text: Sell the pastry concession at the pithead

outcomes.text: You set up a stall between grief and the rope line, and business is excellent, because waiting is hungry work. Head office matches your take with a hospitality grant once they notice you make the crowd look catered rather than desperate. The tea widow stops speaking to you, though she does check your prices twice a day, professionally.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Turn the meeting into a shift roster for the dig

outcomes.text: You stand during questions and read out a dig rota, names and hours, and the room sorts itself around it like filings around a magnet. The deputy assessor watches his meeting become useful and cannot decide whether to object. Gareth hops once along his perch, which the wives take as a yes.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: Half the room follows you out, the other half follows you out slower. In the crush at the door a trestle table takes you across the shins, and the pastries are lost to history. The session is minuted as concluded early due to enthusiasm, and security starts carrying longer torches.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Ask the assessor one question with witnesses present

success.text: You ask, mildly, whether the risk assessment of the rescue also requires a risk assessment, and if so, who assesses that one. The deputy assessor consults his flowchart and finds that it points at itself. To make it stop he grants provisional site access, and the flip chart is quietly turned to a fresh page.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

fail.text: Your question has three clauses and he survives all of them by writing each one down slowly. By the time he finishes, the meeting has run to its scheduled end, which was the plan, though not yours. The crowd files out with pastries and the sense of having been processed.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

Overnight, Bottomless Extraction padlocks the winch house and posts two loss prevention officers on the lift machinery, citing concern for unauthorized wear. The lift is the only way down that does not involve a shovel and a fortnight. A notice on the door explains that the equipment is being protected for the crew's own eventual use, a sentence the tally-keeper reads aloud twice, then once more at a funeral pace. From his cage, Gareth studies the padlock the way managers study a budget line they intend to lose.

choices.text: Ring the winch house with people who will not move

outcomes.text: By noon the winch house is surrounded by a rota so orderly it embarrasses the padlock: widows on chairs, retired lamp-men with sandwiches, a fiddler who takes requests. The loss prevention officers, trained mainly to prevent losses, cannot identify one. Somebody's aunt feeds them anyway, on the reasoning that they look underpaid, which they are.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The night watch of the sit is thinner, and a scuffle at the padlock costs you an elbow and gains the company a photograph of the scuffle. By morning the notice on the door has a second notice about the first notice. The tapping below misses a beat, then resumes, forgiving you.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Serve papers claiming the lift as rescue equipment

outcomes.text: You file to reclassify the lift machinery as emergency apparatus, which under the safety code cannot be locked against its purpose. The magistrate, whose own uncle was a pit man, signs faster than anyone has ever seen him do anything. The padlock comes off with a ceremony nobody planned and everybody attends.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The company's counsel argues that the lift cannot be rescue equipment because no rescue has been approved for it to equip. The magistrate calls this argument disgusting and legally sound. You leave with costs awarded against no one and the padlock developing a shine of permanence.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Cut the padlock and run the winch yourselves

success.text: The bolt cutters go through the padlock like a rumor through a small town. Two old winch-men run the drum by feel, lamps drop into the dark, and for one hour the pit works exactly as designed, without permission. The rescue council disowns the act in public and feeds everyone involved in private.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

fail.text: The cutters slip and the loss prevention officers arrive mid-swing, batons out, apologetic afterward in writing. You wake behind the tea urn with three ribs filing complaints and the padlock promoted to a chain. Head office approves a budget for additional security, effective immediately, unlike the rescue.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

Gareth resigns from management at a quarter past dawn, exiting his executive cage through a door the tally-keeper swears she did not leave open. He is gone down the ventilation gap before anyone can complete an incident report. Two hours later he returns dusty, alive, and carrying in his claw a button from a second-shift coat, at which point the pithead understands it has a route no map admits to. The neighboring pits send word by boy and by pigeon: whatever is needed, ask, invoice later.

choices.text: Turn the bird's route into a surveyed drawing

outcomes.text: An off-duty clerk from the survey office, moonlighting for tea and vengeance, converts Gareth's comings and goings into a proper sectional drawing with a stamp on it. Documents with stamps cannot be ignored by people who worship stamps; head office schedules a site inspection out of pure reflex. The drawing shows an old gallery running forty yards from the trapped face, and now it officially exists.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mine_rescue_crisis Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The drawing is beautiful and the survey office disowns the stamp within the hour, on account of the clerk borrowing it. Head office declares the route hearsay by an unlicensed bird. The gallery goes on existing anyway, quietly, the way facts do while waiting for paperwork to catch up.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mine_rescue_crisis Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Auction the story of the manager bird to the broadsheets

outcomes.text: The broadsheets pay eight for the tale and six more for a portrait of Gareth beside his abandoned cage, captioned executive walks out. The story runs everywhere, and so does a souvenir stall selling little tin canaries. The rescue council notes that you sold their bird, then notes that the tin canaries fund nothing, then stops taking your rounds at the tea urn.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mine_rescue_crisis Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Take the crawl behind Gareth with a rope and a lamp

success.text: You follow the bird into the ventilation gap on your elbows, paying out rope, and reach a fall of stone thin enough to pass soup and profanity through. The first voice through the gap asks, in a coal-dry rasp, whether the surface has completed its paperwork. You laugh so hard you nearly put out the lamp.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mine_rescue_crisis Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

fail.text: The gap narrows until the mountain is wearing you like a sleeve, and you come back short one lamp and a fair amount of skin from both shoulders. Gareth escorts you out at a hover, in the manner of a manager walking a poor performer to the door. The route is real, but it is a route for canaries and children, and nobody is sending children.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mine_rescue_crisis Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

Six days in, the tapping goes economical: shorter messages, longer rests. The company's response schedule promotes the Risk Assessment Review to next quarter, which is head office's way of sending flowers. At the pithead the borehole crew strikes through into the gallery, a hole the width of a soup can, which is not a rescue but is a supply chain. The deputy assessor, off script for the first time in his career, is seen after hours holding the borehole funnel, and holding it correctly.

choices.text: Accept the post of Official Borehole Liaison, salaried

outcomes.text: Bottomless Extraction, needing the supply hole to look like their idea, salaries you to stand beside it in a company sash, eight a week plus six for the sash photograph. You change nothing about how the soup goes down, only who is described as providing it. The families keep using the hole and stop using your name.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_compromise Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: Bottomless Extraction, needing the supply hole to look like their idea, salaries you to stand beside it in a company sash, eight a week plus six for the sash photograph. You change nothing about how the soup goes down, only who is described as providing it. The families keep using the hole and stop using your name.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_compromise Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Run the soup line day and night, in shifts

success.text: The tea widow designs a menu for men who cannot see it: hot, salty, and narrow. Every four hours the funnel swallows broth, candle stubs, and one written joke, selected by committee for load-bearing quality. Gareth supervises from the funnel housing, head tilted, auditing the broth. The tapping picks up its tempo, and the old men translate the new rhythm with wet eyes: soup received, send worse jokes.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_compromise Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

fail.text: You fall asleep standing at the funnel on the third night and pour a ladle of broth down your own boot, scalding a foot that had done nothing wrong. The line holds because the rota holds, not because you do. The tea widow sends you to bed with the voice she otherwise reserves for the company.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_compromise Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Invoke the clause that trapped men are still on shift

outcomes.text: You produce the company's own handbook: an employee is on shift until signed out at the lamp room, and no one below has signed out. On shift men are owed wages, meal provision, and a safe route of egress, a phrase you read aloud slowly, twice. Payroll, terrified of arrears, becomes the first department to formally endorse a rescue, and requisitions the soup as a deductible meal service.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_compromise Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The handbook citation ricochets: the company agrees the men are on shift and docks them for the tools lost in the collapse. The clerks are ashamed, the crowd is incandescent, and somewhere a filing drawer eats the arrears claim. Even the deputy assessor calls it low, off the record, into his tea.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_compromise Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

On the ninth morning the company offers terms in a tent with carpet in it. Bottomless Extraction will permit a full rescue, using the lift machinery, provided every rescuer signs a waiver acknowledging the rescue is a voluntary recreational activity, unrelated to mining, undertaken against advice. The deputy assessor reads the terms aloud, and his voice does something strange at the word recreational, like a boot going through wet floorboards. Gareth attends perched on the tent pole, above everyone's authority, technically outdoors.

choices.text: Sign the waivers together and go down anyway

outcomes.text: The rescue council signs as one body, in alphabetical order, every signature legible out of pure spite. Recreational or not, the lift drops, and the first crews go down singing something with too many verses. The waiver stack gets a rock put on it so the wind cannot take the company's victory anywhere useful.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_reversal Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_reversal Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: You sign, you descend, and eleven feet down the recreational activity drops a stone the size of an oath on your collarbone. Up top the company notes the injury proves their risk projections, and sends a fruit basket addressed to the activity participant. The tea widow eats the fruit and keeps the basket for eggs.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_reversal Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Redline the waiver until it points back at them

success.text: You initial every clause and add one: participants recreate at the site solely because the operator failed to maintain it, see attached survey. The company lawyer reads it, goes the color of an unpaid invoice, and withdraws the word recreational by supper. What remains is a plain rescue authorization on carpet-tent letterhead, which everyone agrees is the finest thing the tent produced.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_reversal Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

fail.text: Your amendment war lasts four hours and produces a document neither side can sign without a headache. The tent is folded, the carpet rolled, the terms tabled. The only party satisfied is the borehole funnel, which continues to accept soup without terms and conditions.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_reversal Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Go through the company's site records after dark

success.text: The records shed lock answers to a hairpin and an apology. Inside you find the original survey, the one showing a second egress tunnel sealed in a cost review, filed under Discontinued Conveniences. You pass copies to the magistrate and keep the finder's fee his court quietly pays for material evidence; the rescue council thanks you and still counts the spoons when you visit.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_reversal Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

fail.text: You find the sealed-tunnel survey and sell your first look at it to a broadsheet before the magistrate gets his. Eight up front, and the headline does travel; it also warns the company, whose men are seen at the old tunnel mouth by dawn with fresh timber and no comment. The crowd's mood curdles from patient to flinty, and your tea now arrives without a saucer.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_reversal Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

A junior clerk, checking signatures for the waiver file, makes the discovery of her career: the promotion that made Gareth regional manager was never revoked, and regional managers hold Class Two signing authority, including Form RA-77. There is precedent for a mark in lieu of signature, and the precedent does not specify a species, because the drafters never imagined a canary would defect. The pithead goes very quiet while the deputy assessor confirms it against the handbook. He closes the book gently, and for the first time all fortnight, he smiles like a man off duty.

choices.text: Put the bird's footprint on Form RA-77, witnessed

outcomes.text: Gareth steps in the ink pad with the gravity of a signing ceremony, then walks the length of the form, approving it more than once, twice on the third page. Class Two authority in wet black prints: the risk assessment of the rescue is complete, filed, and unappealable without insulting the company's own org chart. The lift machinery is running before the ink dries, because this time the paperwork is on the side with shovels.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_legacy Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: Head office contests the footprint on grounds of species, and the question goes to a tribunal that has never moved fast and will not start for a bird. The form sits certified pending appeal, a phrase that means both yes and no. Still, the clerks now file every company motion at the bottom of the tray, by accident, repeatedly.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_legacy Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Sell stamped souvenir copies of the footprint form

outcomes.text: You run off facsimiles of the signed RA-77, footprints and all, and the pithead crowd buys them faster than you can stamp, eight in the first hour, six more by dusk. Head office buys a dozen through an intermediary, for reasons nobody wants to guess at. The rescue council observes that the original was not yours to duplicate; the observation is filed under things that are true.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_legacy Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Stand the rescue teams up before the appeal lands

outcomes.text: You spend your own coin on lamp oil and rope so no requisition can slow the muster, and by nightfall three teams stand ready at the lift in borrowed helmets. Whatever the tribunals decide, the pit will present them with a finished rescue to rule on. Gareth inspects the line like the manager he never asked to be, and finds it good.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_legacy Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: Mustering in the dark, you walk into the winch house door hard enough to be minuted, and the teams lose an hour to laughing carefully at you. The line forms anyway, crooked and certain. The appeal, wherever it is, does not know what is coming up out of the ground to meet it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_legacy Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

Eleven men come up on the lift machinery in the grey of morning, thinner, filthy, and complaining about the jokes, which is how the tally-keeper knows they are whole. She wipes no names; she adds the date beside each in small chalk, returned. Bottomless Extraction issues a statement crediting the successful outcome to robust internal process, and in a sense this is accurate, since the process it drowned in was its own. Gareth declines reinstatement to management and accepts, by show of hands, the new post of shaft safety officer, answerable to nobody above ground, salary payable in millet and respect.

choices.text: Publish your memoir of the rescue, exclusive rights

outcomes.text: The broadsheets pay eight for serialization and six for the chapter where you personally follow a bird into a mountain, lightly rewritten so you go first. The eleven read it aloud below ground on slow shifts, doing your voice, apparently. You are welcome at the tea urn on the understanding that certain chapters are never mentioned while the widow is pouring.

choices.text: Stay on and rebuild the rescue rota into a fixture

outcomes.text: You stay past the celebrations, which is when staying counts, and help fold the dig rota into a permanent watch: lamps checked, ropes counted, one seat at every meeting reserved for whoever is currently underground. The handbook gains a line the clerks smuggled in during the appeal chaos: no ore shall be priced above a miner's life, filed under general provisions where nobody will ever think to delete it. Gareth signs the page, in ink, with his foot.

The handbook gains a line the clerks smuggled in during the appeal chaos: no ore shall be priced above a miner's life, filed under general provisions where nobody will ever think to delete it. You stay to help take down the winch house scaffolding, and the scaffolding registers its objection across your back. The eleven carry you to the tea urn on a plank, in state, laughing. The watch forms anyway, and your plank is retired to the meeting room as furniture with a service record.

choices.text: Write the incident report head office must frame

success.text: You draft the official account in flawless head office prose: timelines, learnings, a commitment to continuous improvement, and, in the appendix, the footprinted RA-77 reproduced at full size. Head office adopts it whole, because it reads like their own voice, and thereby certifies for the permanent record that a canary out-managed them. The deputy assessor requests a framed copy for his office and hangs it where visitors will ask.

fail.text: Your report is accepted with revisions, and the revisions remove the bird, the padlock, the tunnel, and most of the verbs. What survives is a paragraph thanking all stakeholders. The pithead keeps its own account, told at the urn, growing one honest exaggeration per winter.

text:
SEED VAULT

At six in the morning a process server nails a summons to the vault door and reads charges aloud to a turnip in an evidence bag. AgriFuture Patent Cathedral alleges intellectual theft: the defendant, it claims, infringes Patent 4401, A Novel Rounded Root, and has been infringing since before the Cathedral existed, which the filing calls aggravating. The head seed librarian alphabetizes furiously, which is what she does instead of screaming. The turnip says nothing, and will hold this position with distinction.

choices.text: Sell the Cathedral a map of the vault's unlicensed shelves

outcomes.text: The Cathedral's associate counsel pays in crisp notes and apologizes twice while counting them out, which he seems to believe makes it neighborly. By noon there are inventory clerks in the aisles wearing soft armor and stickering everything round, and the terraces have noticed. The librarians watch you leave through the good door, the one they no longer hold open for you. The turnip, bagged on the cart, does not judge you. That is a kindness you have not earned.
effects: schedule 2 r18_seed_vault_assembly The turnip awaits its verdict and says nothing

choices.text: Stand between the evidence cart and the vault door

success.text: You put one hand on the evidence cart and announce that the accused is entitled to counsel, which nobody present can immediately disprove. The head librarian stops alphabetizing, deputizes you with a rubber stamp, and hangs a lanyard on you still warm from the laminator. The process server takes down everyone's names, including the defendant's, which it turns out nobody knows. You are now, formally, part of the defense.

Injured during filing, she says, counts double.

choices.text: Demand a proper arraignment and bail for the accused

Somewhere in the Patent Cathedral an accountant opens a line item called defendant, upkeep of, and feels a chill he cannot bill for.

The head librarian thanks you for trying, with the face of a woman revising her estimate of the law downward.

text:
SEED VAULT

Word travels the terraces the old way, over fences, and by evening the reading room is full of grandmothers with tins. The head librarian wants prior art: recipe cards, wedding photographs, anything with the defendant in it and a date on it. The widow from the terrace allotments arrives last, carrying her recipe tin the way other people carry urns. The turnip has been assigned a shelf in the evidence room, a case number, and, by the stenographer of Chamber Nine, the benefit of the doubt.

choices.text: Canvass the allotments for cards and photographs

success.text: You go fence to fence with a borrowed satchel and come back with sixty one recipe cards, nine wedding photographs, and a promise from a family upriver to look for a christening notice. In one photograph the turnip sits garlanded at the head table, nearer the couple than the groom's own mother, dated decades before the patent. The reading room votes you the good satchel. It has a strap and everything.
effects: schedule 3 r18_seed_vault_retaliation The turnip awaits its verdict and says nothing

fail.text: The widow's neighbor mistakes you for a Cathedral man and defends her seed drawer with a trowel and real conviction. You come back with the cards, a bandaged forearm, and an apology jar of pickle, which around here is a binding treaty. The head librarian logs your wound in the accession book. Everything in this building gets catalogued eventually.

choices.text: Number every tin into a court ready exhibit index

outcomes.text: You spend the night cross referencing tins against dates, and by morning there is an index proving the defendant predates Patent 4401 by four generations of Sunday dinners. The clerk of Chamber Nine accepts it with the reverence of a man handed somebody else's finished homework. Even the Cathedral's associate counsel requests a copy, for admiration, he says, which is not a legal category but should be.

text:
SEED VAULT

Thursday brings retaliation on Cathedral letterhead: sixty cease and desist letters, one per grandmother, alleging unlicensed propagation of a patented root by means of soup. A second motion asks the court to pulp the defendant early, citing storage costs. The associate counsel delivers every letter personally and apologizes at every door, keeping a tally of his apologies in a small book. In the evidence room, the chain of custody form, read closely, obliges somebody to water the accused.

choices.text: Move to quash all sixty letters with one filing

success.text: You argue before Chamber Nine that soup is consumption, not propagation, and that no root in recorded history has been multiplied by simmering. The judge, who has eaten soup, rules from experience and quashes all sixty letters in a single order. The motion to pulp is held over, deferred until the court can decide whether a defendant can be both evidence and compost. It cannot, you suspect, but suspense is billable.
effects: schedule 4 r18_seed_vault_coalition The turnip awaits its verdict and says nothing

fail.text: Chamber Nine finds your filing persuasive but one signature short of valid, the missing signature being the defendant's, who declines comment. The letters stand and the terraces simmer, which the Cathedral's lawyers should honestly log as a propagation risk. The grandmothers frame their letters and hang them over their stoves. You fought on paper and lost on paper, which around here still counts as fighting.
effects: schedule 4 r18_seed_vault_coalition The turnip awaits its verdict and says nothing

choices.text: Pull the Cathedral's original patent filing

success.text: In the patent archive you find the original filing for 4401, and the botanical drawing is traced, down to a scar on the taproot matching a photograph already in evidence. A broadsheet pays four coins for the story and runs it under a woodcut of the Cathedral looking guilty, which architecture can apparently do. The head librarian is livid you fed the papers before the court. Truth has a filing order, apparently, and you jumped the queue.
effects: schedule 4 r18_seed_vault_coalition The turnip awaits its verdict and says nothing

fail.text: The Cathedral's archivist finds you in the stacks holding the original filing and offers a consulting fee with a nondisclosure stapled to it. You sign, the fee is generous, and the tracing you definitely saw becomes a tracing you cannot discuss, even with yourself. On your way out the archivist locks the drawer with a little smile. Somewhere behind you, a librarian's opinion of you quietly reshelves itself.
effects: schedule 4 r18_seed_vault_coalition The turnip awaits its verdict and says nothing

choices.text: Start a watering rota for the prisoner

outcomes.text: Chain of custody, it turns out, cuts both ways: whoever holds the evidence must maintain the evidence, and the form never says who may volunteer. By the weekend there is a rota, a watering can with a court sticker on it, and a guard who has stopped pretending the biscuits are not for him. The associate counsel signs for a Tuesday slot under a false name that everyone recognizes. The turnip thrives, admissibly.

SEED VAULT

Allies arrive uninvited, which is how you know they are real: bakers, picklers, two rival millers observing a truce, and a christening family from upriver who swear the defendant attended their daughter's naming and have the register to prove it. The defense kitty lives in a biscuit tin with a slot cut in the lid. The stenographer attends strategy suppers on her own time, insisting she is only present for accuracy. In custody, the turnip has put out one green shoot, which the Cathedral's lawyers are pretending not to see.

choices.text: License your defense notes to a rival patent house

outcomes.text: A rival patent house pays lavishly for your notes on the defense, planning to sue the Cathedral over the same root and own it themselves once the dust settles. You have not saved the turnip; you have widened the auction. The rival's clerk shakes your hand with both of his, which is how you know to count your fingers. Out on the terraces the kitty tin rattles on without you, lighter and prouder.

text:
SEED VAULT

An emergency session convenes because one shoot has become three. The Cathedral petitions for summary destruction, arguing the defendant is now infringing in real time, copying itself in front of witnesses, and must be pulped within two days. The head librarian faints briefly into the card catalog and files herself under F. The turnip stands accused of the only crime a turnip can commit with enthusiasm.

choices.text: Hold an overnight vigil at the evidence room

outcomes.text: By dusk the evidence room steps hold forty folding chairs, eleven thermoses, and a wall of grandmothers doing needlework in shifts, facing the door. The marshal sent to secure the corridor surveys the field, accepts a blanket, and reports back that the perimeter is holding but is mostly theirs now. No van comes. Vans, it turns out, have a strong instinct about grandmothers.

The pulping crew, unloaded and stared at by the returning vigil, elects to become a delivery crew instead.

Chamber Nine grants the stay and, warming to the theme, orders the new shoots watered at the movant's expense. Somewhere in the Cathedral an accountant reopens the line item, defendant, upkeep of, and watches it bud.

The order is somewhere in Chamber Nine, legally alive and physically lost. You have not won. You have merely made the paperwork mortal too.

For a month, every soup tastes faintly of clause.

SEED VAULT

Settlement papers arrive perfumed, a Cathedral tradition: the defendant may live, provided the vault accepts a license, every grandmother registers her recipes, and royalties accrue per serving. The associate counsel presents the terms like a man showing off his own gallows, proud of the joinery. The widow reads all forty pages without her glasses, out of contempt. In the evidence room the turnip has five shoots and, the stenographer's margin notes record, a following.

choices.text: Counter with back royalties on their canteen soup

outcomes.text: You table a counterclaim: the Cathedral's staff canteen has served the widow's turnip soup for eleven years, recipe lifted whole from a parish cookbook, back royalties due at the Cathedral's own proposed rate. Their accountants run the figure, go the color of the soup, and request a recess. The settlement leaves the table at the speed of arithmetic. The widow asks for her counterclaim framed, and gets it.

By dawn the defendant has toured the terraces in a bicycle basket, been blessed by two separate congregations, and returned to its shelf repotted, and nobody can prove the soil is new. The head librarian calls it evidence tampering; the terraces call it a homecoming. The record calls it nothing, because the stenographer was, at the crucial hour, fetching tea.

text:
SEED VAULT

The christening register cracks the case on a Tuesday. There in the guest column, thirty one years back, sits the Cathedral's founding botanist, signature and all, seated beside a turnip listed as honored guest, of the variety Doris's Winter Round, named for the widow's late mother in law. He left, the register notes, with most of the cake and a cutting. Patent 4401 did not invent the defendant. It attended the defendant's christening and took notes.

choices.text: Auction the register's story to the broadsheets

outcomes.text: The broadsheets bid against each other in your kitchen, and the winner prints the register across the front page under a woodcut of a smug root. By the time court convenes the revelation is old news and its thunder is spent, though the verdict will likely survive your scoop. The widow's family history is now a souvenir. The fee is handsome, and the woodcut, you are told, is selling separately.

Cross examination attempts to shake a woman who has survived four decades of bake sale politics, and it goes the way you would expect. Chamber Nine is very quiet, except for the knitting.

The judge asks whether the defendant truly attended. Honored guest, says the register, and now the record says it twice.

text:
SEED VAULT

Verdict day fills Chamber Nine past the fire code, which the marshal waives, citing a precedent he invents on the spot. Acquitted, on grounds of prior art: the court finds the defendant has been itself since before the Cathedral existed, and that being yourself, while frequently actionable, is not infringement. Costs to the Cathedral, including the water bill. The turnip receives the verdict in silence, having found a strategy that works, and the stenographer enters that silence into the record one last time, then applauds.

choices.text: Plant the shoots along the courthouse steps

success.text: The planting takes all afternoon: shoots along the courthouse steps, shoots to the terraces, the eldest shoot to the upriver family, who have opinions about godparents. The turnip itself retires to the vault as Emeritus Specimen, catalog card written in the head librarian's best hand, visitors welcome. The associate counsel resigns from the Cathedral and takes the Tuesday watering slot under his real name, which turns out to be lovely and beside the point. The vault charter now lists you as Keeper of the Rota, and seed travels the terraces free of royalties, which the landlords experience as a personal insult.

fail.text: The courthouse soil is mostly rubble and old verdicts, and your back gives out on the third hole. You are carried to the tea urn like a fallen marshal while grandmothers finish the row with insulting ease. The shoots go in anyway, every one, and the head librarian catalogs your lumbago as a founding injury. The charter lists you as Keeper of the Rota, planting duties waived on medical grounds, honor undiminished.

choices.text: Rewrite the verdict as a form any kitchen can cite

outcomes.text: You distill the ruling into a one page form, the Prior Art Declaration, sized to fit inside a recipe tin lid, with a line for the dish and a line for the grandmother. The clerk of Chamber Nine stocks it beside the marriage forms, where it outsells them. Patent law, having spent a season prosecuting a vegetable, now spends its retirement defending kitchens. The turnip's catalog card carries the first stamped copy, folded, like a service medal.

Your form is a triumph of layout, which is the problem: the Cathedral reprints the design with the meaning reversed, and for a week nobody can tell the shield from the invoice. The clerk recalls both versions and issues a third bearing a watermark of the defendant, which cannot be faked because nobody else has the woodcut. Order returns, slower than deserved. The turnip has become the security feature. It would be proud, if it did that.

choices.text: Trademark the acquitted's likeness for souvenirs

outcomes.text: You file for the likeness before the grandmothers think to, and by market day there are tea towels, woodcuts, and a commemorative pickling jar bearing the face of the acquitted. The vault receives nothing per unit, which is what per the licensing schedule means. Rent on the terraces ticks upward, because everything popular becomes somebody's justification. The turnip, emeritus in its drawer, will outlast every tea towel. Roots generally do.

text:
MOON LANDLORD

The invoice arrives on day nine of the two-week night, printed on paper that warms itself, which is not a courtesy, it is so the ink can move. Lunar Shelter Assets has billed Dome Row for shadow occupancy, vacuum upkeep, and one amenity listed only as gravity, per Schedule G of the lease. The night-shift airlock warden reads it twice and quietly logs it as a hazard. In Dome Nine, the retired ore hauler weighs the envelope in one hand and says it will not be the heaviest thing in this crater for long.

choices.text: Start a kitty for the filing fee

outcomes.text: You put in three coins and the tin goes dome to dome by airlock, which on the moon means every donation is preceded by four minutes of etiquette. By morning shift the kitty holds enough for the filing fee and a spare gasket, because somebody always puts in a gasket. The warden logs you as a friend of the row. Schedule G, pinned to the notice board, curls one corner as if listening.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_assembly Rent comes due on the shadows again

outcomes.text: The tin makes it to Dome Four before the pressure hatch shuts on your fingers, which the etiquette handbook classes as your fault for hurrying. The kitty comes back light and the row comes back loud. Still, everyone now knows the words shadow occupancy, and says them the way you would say a rash. Schedule G stays warm on the board, digesting.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_assembly Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: File a query about the shadow line item

outcomes.text: The dispute board meets in the one room with a window, and the window is the whole agenda until your query arrives. You ask, in writing, who manufactured the shadows and when they were installed. The clerk stamps it RECEIVED with visible pleasure; the board has wanted a reason to dislike this invoice since it learned to read. Lunar Shelter Assets is given ten days to identify its shadow supplier.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_assembly Rent comes due on the shadows again

outcomes.text: Your query comes back appended to Schedule G, which has ruled that shadow is a fixture cast by company property, and fixtures bill by the square meter. Somewhere in the wording your filing became evidence that you use shadow, which you do, daily, like a criminal. The clerk stamps it RECEIVED anyway, softer this time.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_assembly Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Take the shadow surveyor job

outcomes.text: Lunar Shelter Assets is hiring shadow surveyors, local applicants preferred, no experience with sunlight necessary. You spend a shift walking Dome Row with a lumen wand, measuring your neighbors' shade at fourteen coins the survey. The measurements are excellent. That is somehow the worst part, and the warden's logbook records your boots by name.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_assembly Rent comes due on the shadows again

text:
MOON LANDLORD

Sixty tenants fit in the window room if nobody exhales with ambition. The board has ruled that before anything else, Schedule G must be read aloud in full, because a document that has never been spoken has never really been met. The warden carries it in on a tray, wearing gloves. Outside the window the moon does exactly nothing, which the ore hauler says is the only honest landlord's response the crater has ever gotten.

choices.text: Read Schedule G aloud, with footnotes

success.text: You read all forty minutes of it, pausing at the clause that defines gravity as an amenity, provided at grade, subject to seasonal adjustment. You let the silence do the arithmetic: the moon has one season, and nobody here ordered it. By clause thirty the room is laughing in the flat, dangerous way of people reading their own bill. The board enters the recital into the record, and Schedule G is never quite as warm afterward.
effects: schedule 4 r18_moon_landlord_retaliation Rent comes due on the shadows again

fail.text: You read it aloud and the document wins the room, because legalese read at speed sounds like authority to anyone raised on announcements. Two tenants leave convinced they do owe for shadow and should settle early. The clerk enters the recital into the record and the record visibly sides with the paper. The warden logs the evening under weather: worse.
effects: schedule 4 r18_moon_landlord_retaliation Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Sell the attendance sheet to the adjustor

outcomes.text: The adjustor from Lunar Shelter Assets waits in the corridor and pays fourteen coins for the attendance sheet, which he calls a courtesy copy. He is gone before the applause. When the row finds out, the meeting stops being about the invoice and starts being about the airlock codes, and you cannot entirely blame the paper for that.
effects: schedule 4 r18_moon_landlord_retaliation Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Set a rota so every dome reads a clause

outcomes.text: Forty clauses, forty voices, one per dome, and the mushroom vault sends a child who reads the indemnity section with terrifying accuracy. By the end the Schedule has been handled by everyone it bills, and it knows it. The board declares the reading a standing tradition, pending nothing. People trade clauses in the corridor afterward like a card game with no winner, which is the correct reading of the lease.
effects: schedule 4 r18_moon_landlord_retaliation Rent comes due on the shadows again

outcomes.text: The rota holds until clause nineteen, when two domes both claim the definitions section and you take an elbow in the eye settling it, moon gravity making the swing slow and therefore extremely well documented. The reading finishes ragged. Even so, every dome now owns one memorized clause, and a memorized clause is a splinter the paper cannot bill for.
effects: schedule 4 r18_moon_landlord_retaliation Rent comes due on the shadows again

text:
MOON LANDLORD

Amenity withdrawal notices go up at first light, meaning a technician with a torch, since the sun is a week away. Delinquent domes are advised that gravity service will be suspended pending payment, effective the fourteenth. The technician will not say how. The warden asks whether it will be suspended upward, and the technician writes down the question like a threat, and Schedule G grows a rider overnight to cover it.

choices.text: Block the bailiff crawler at the south lock

success.text: Eleven tenants and one borrowed cargo sledge make the south lock a fact the crawler has to negotiate with. The bailiffs consult their manual, which covers resistance but not parking, and withdraw pending clarification. The row cheers; the board does not, officially, being a board. On the notice board Schedule G's new rider curls away from the window, as if it dislikes being seen from outside.
effects: schedule 5 r18_moon_landlord_coalition Rent comes due on the shadows again

fail.text: The crawler has a policy for you, and the policy is called a winch. You are moved gently, in sixth gravity, a long slow arc that gives you time to consider the lease and still land badly. Lunar Shelter Assets logs the incident as amenity abuse, tenant-side. The bailiffs now come in pairs, and the pairs come with helmets.
effects: schedule 5 r18_moon_landlord_coalition Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Take in the family from the tagged dome

outcomes.text: Dome Six absorbs a family of four, two chickens of unclear paperwork, and a kettle with a strong personality. It is crowded in the way that keeps people warm at nothing per hour, a rate Schedule G has no field for. The warden amends the occupancy log with the single word: guests. The tagged dome sits empty and billed, and somehow the emptiness is on the company now.
effects: schedule 5 r18_moon_landlord_coalition Rent comes due on the shadows again

outcomes.text: Four extra sleepers means the air scrubber sulks, and you take the cot beside it and wake with the headache the manual calls advisory. The kettle screams at shift change; tempers follow it. But the family stays, and the row learns the tagged notices are survivable, which was the one thing the notices needed nobody to know.
effects: schedule 5 r18_moon_landlord_coalition Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Demand the mechanism of gravity suspension

outcomes.text: Your filing asks Lunar Shelter Assets to specify the tools, staffing, and safety protocol by which gravity will be discontinued. Ten days pass. The reply, when it comes, cites a subcontractor, then a second subcontractor, then a firm that turns out to make doorbells. The board reads the chain aloud in the window room and even the clerk laughs, once, in writing.
effects: schedule 5 r18_moon_landlord_coalition Rent comes due on the shadows again

outcomes.text: The reply arrives inside a fresh copy of Schedule G, which now defines suspension of service as any period during which the tenant worries about the service. By that measure the suspension began weeks ago and you owe a reconnection fee. You have been billed for dread. The clerk stamps it RECEIVED at arm's length.
effects: schedule 5 r18_moon_landlord_coalition Rent comes due on the shadows again

text:
MOON LANDLORD

An ice crew from the north rim arrives mid-shift with a sledge of frozen water and a copy of their own lease, which is somehow worse. The mushroom vaults send a delegate who smells like dinner and negotiates like winter. Everyone wants to see Schedule G, which now travels in a biscuit tin, for containment. Word is Lunar Shelter Assets has been quietly rewriting every lease on the rim to match it, clause by clause, like a cough going around.

choices.text: Pool rations and air with the rim crews

success.text: The arithmetic takes an evening: ice for mushrooms, mushrooms for filter time, filter time for the loan of a welder, and no line of it billable, because none of it is a transaction, it is a rota with soup. The vault delegate calls it a shared umbrella, has the term explained, and likes it more afterward. Nobody on the rim skips a meal that month. Schedule G rattles in its tin whenever the sledges pass, unpaid.
effects: schedule 2 r18_moon_landlord_crisis Rent comes due on the shadows again

fail.text: The pooling works except for the manifest, which you volunteer to carry between craters, and which weighs nothing and wrenches your shoulder anyway, because the sledge does not. Two domes accuse each other of counting mushrooms creatively. The soup happens regardless. On the moon, a grudge and a rota can share a table for years.
effects: schedule 2 r18_moon_landlord_crisis Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Hunt the oldest lease on the rim

outcomes.text: In the ice crew's archive box, under a dead man's mitten, is a survey-era lease from before Lunar Shelter Assets existed, and it defines shadow as a natural encumbrance, borne by the ground, at no charge to any party. Grandmother clauses do not expire, they nap. The board certifies the old page and pins it beside Schedule G, which spends the night as far from it as the cork allows.
effects: schedule 2 r18_moon_landlord_crisis Rent comes due on the shadows again

outcomes.text: You find the old survey lease and read it aloud too soon, in the corridor, without the board present, which legally makes it an anecdote. By the time it is filed, Lunar Shelter Assets has produced an amendment predated to the survey era, on paper that is suspiciously good at looking tired. The clerk accepts both documents and files them facing away from each other.
effects: schedule 2 r18_moon_landlord_crisis Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Tip the adjustor to the archive box

outcomes.text: The adjustor is buying old leases, cash, no questions, preservation purposes. You know where the ice crew keeps their archive box, and fourteen coins is fourteen coins. The box crosses the crater at night on a sledge you did not exactly drive. The rim crews recount their papers in the morning, and the vault delegate stops smelling like dinner when you walk in.
effects: schedule 2 r18_moon_landlord_crisis Rent comes due on the shadows again

text:
MOON LANDLORD

Rider K is discovered at dawn shift stapled to Schedule G, though nobody owns a stapler and the tin was shut. It announces retroactive billing for gravity enjoyed since each tenant's date of birth, compounding, payable in this life or a documented next one. The ore hauler, sixty-two years heavy, is invoiced a sum that has its own appendix. The window room fills before the kettle does, and the moon outside stays level and gray, unconcerned, holding everyone down for free.

choices.text: File an emergency stay against Rider K

outcomes.text: The board grants the stay in eleven minutes, a rim record, on the grounds that a bill served on the day of birth requires a signature nobody can produce. Rider K is suspended pending review and pinned under glass where it can be watched. Lunar Shelter Assets protests that documents cannot be caged. The clerk replies, in writing, that this one can.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_compromise Rent comes due on the shadows again

outcomes.text: The stay is denied on a technicality: your filing said gravity where the rider says gravitational amenity, and the board must rule on the paper as served. You have lost on a definition, which in this crater is like drowning indoors. The clerk stamps DENIED, and then, off the record, underlines your best paragraph in pencil.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_compromise Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Sell a short list of names

outcomes.text: Lunar Shelter Assets wants the names of tenants keeping unregistered survey papers, and pays fourteen coins for a short list. You write four names, none spelled entirely correctly, which is the flag you plant for your own conscience. The visits happen anyway. In the corridor, the warden's logbook acquires a page with your name at the top and nothing after it, which is worse than anything it could have said.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_compromise Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Sit with Dome Nine when the collectors come

success.text: The collectors arrive to inventory the ore hauler's possessions against her arrears and find forty tenants sitting on them. Everything in the dome is, at present, in use: the chair, the wrench, the second chair, the floor. Confiscation of items in use requires form after form, and the collectors have brought one pen. They leave with a doormat, by consent, and the row waves them off like a ferry.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_compromise Rent comes due on the shadows again

fail.text: The sit lasts six hours before your knee gives out on a crate of hauling tackle, and the collectors, embarrassed, help you up and then impound the crate you were guarding. The row jeers them down the corridor. The ore hauler keeps her kettle and her chair, loses her tackle, and tells you a knee given in company is a fair price, which does not make the walk home shorter.
effects: schedule 3 r18_moon_landlord_compromise Rent comes due on the shadows again

text:
MOON LANDLORD

Lunar Shelter Assets sends terms in a folder that hisses when opened, having been pressurized at head office. The offer: all gravity arrears forgiven, Rider K withdrawn, in exchange for a perpetual shadow rate signed by every dome. The adjustor calls it generosity. The warden reads the perpetuity clause twice and notes that the only other perpetual thing on the moon is the moon.

choices.text: Dig into who actually holds the crater title

success.text: Three nights in the survey archive and you find it: Lunar Shelter Assets does not own the crater, it subleases it from a trust that dissolved decades ago, meaning the landlord is itself a tenant whose landlord is deceased. You invoice the board four coins for archival labor, which the clerk pays while frowning at the precedent. The row toasts you; the board notes, in the minutes, that heroes do not usually attach receipts.
effects: schedule 4 r18_moon_landlord_reversal Rent comes due on the shadows again

fail.text: You find the dead trust and the broken title, and the adjustor finds you finding it, and offers eight coins for the folder while it is still warm. Callable research, he says. You take it. The finding surfaces anyway a week later, through the ice crew, minus your name; the row learns you had it first, and the kettle in Dome Six stops boiling on your visits, which chemically should not be possible.
effects: schedule 4 r18_moon_landlord_reversal Rent comes due on the shadows again

choices.text: Vote dome by dome, no dome left unasked

outcomes.text: The vote takes four days because the far domes get visits, not ballots: tea, the terms read aloud, and time to be angry in a chair. The count is forty to nothing against perpetuity, with one abstention from a chicken. The adjustor receives the result stapled, which he understands as the insult it is. Nobody signed anything, and the row walks heavier for it, in the good way.
effects: schedule 4 r18_moon_landlord_reversal Rent comes due on the shadows again
