text:
HEALER STRIKE

Judgment day at the tribunal, which the Temple keeps calling an adjudication event. The finding is read in full liturgical voice: the concept of Tuesday is ruled medically necessary, being a day on which patients are reliably alive, and all care rendered on or adjacent to a Tuesday, a category that survey confirms includes every day, is preauthorized in perpetuity. The surgeon receives four centuries of interest, and the Temple receives her next invoice. The free ward has become, by precedent, the billing standard.

choices.text: Read the Temple its own new liturgy, with feeling

success.text: You take the lectern and perform the finding the way the Temple performs fees, incense, drone and all, so the vergers can absorb it in their native register. By the second refrain the intake desk is unlocking doors and calling it doctrine. The deacon of copays, retitled deacon of payouts, weeps openly and stamps APPROVED in time with the cadence. The fee schedule is retired to the archive, where it can be studied like any other plague.

fail.text: You lose the cadence in stanza three, and a verger, moved by pure muscle memory, denies an old woman at the door before catching himself. The finding stands, but the crier reports that the new order sounds shaky, and pressure returns like a phantom fee. The surgeon takes the lectern after you and lands it, having rehearsed on every Tuesday she was ever denied.

choices.text: Bill the tribunal for witnessing history

outcomes.text: Attendance at a precedent, you argue, is a professional service rendered to the future, and the clerk, newly terrified of denying anything, pays fourteen coins on the spot. It simply works; under the new order, absurd claims get paid, and you have moved to the winning side of the absurdity. The matron observes that some people learned exactly one thing from the surgeon. The surgeon, asked for comment, requests a copy of your invoice for her collection.

choices.text: Take the Tuesday shift at the free ward, permanently

outcomes.text: The rota hangs where the fee schedule used to, laminated, because permanence was a thing the Temple could always do, just never for this. Your name sits in the Tuesday column beside the surgeon's, who is drafting her next claim: the concept of rest, submitted on behalf of the night staff, prognosis hopeful. The tin retires to a shelf above the door under a small plaque, PAID IN FULL, INDEFINITELY. On Tuesdays, which is to say always, the dying come in free, and the machine that priced them now writes it down and pays.

You take the shift and the shift takes its tithe: the free ward under precedent is busier than the paid ward ever dared to be, and by week's end you have donated a back muscle to the cause of moving cots. The district, given something that works, immediately demands more of it, loudly, which the matron calls a healthy sign and the vergers call unrest. The surgeon splints your back and files your claim herself, first in the queue for Tuesday. It is approved before you finish wincing.

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Seven o'clock, parents' evening, and the gymnasium smells of floor polish and dread. A man in a blazer announces that FutureMinds Private Academy has acquired the school, effective immediately, students included as fixtures. Each family receives a tuition invoice containing three variables, a summation sign, and a footnote that cites itself. The deputy head in charge of laminating stands beside him holding the sign-in clipboard like a shield. In the front row, a nine year old named Prudence has already started solving it.

choices.text: Demand the invoice reissued in plain figures

outcomes.text: You stand on a tiny chair and cite the Legibility of Charges Act, which requires any bill to be readable by the person it ruins. The blazer man's smile holds while his clerk confirms that yes, the Act exists, and yes, a summation sign counts as concealment. FutureMinds is ordered to reissue in plain figures, a defeat they log as customer feedback. The deputy head lends you the clipboard so you can sign out, respectfully.

The invoice, he rules, is not a bill but a learning resource, and learning resources are permitted to be impossible. The gymnasium deflates. FutureMinds adds a small fee for the hearing, payable in the same notation.

choices.text: Take the Revenue Ambassador lanyard and the cut

outcomes.text: The blazer man takes you aside by the wall bars and offers you a lanyard that says REVENUE AMBASSADOR. You will collect tuition door to door, keeping a percentage, plus a signing bonus paid on the spot in a school envelope. The money is real, the envelope smells of poster paint, and the parents watch you pocket it with expressions you decide not to collect. At the gate, two new guards wave you through like a colleague.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_assembly Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Push the tiny tables together and compare invoices

success.text: You push four tiny tables together and ask every family to lay its invoice down. No two are alike: same school, same children, sums that differ by hundreds, one printed upside down. By nine o'clock there is a rota for checking each other's arithmetic and a biscuit tin with a slot cut in the lid for the legal fund. Prudence takes minutes, in ink, unasked.

The rota forms anyway, around your ankle, which is now part of the story.

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Monday assembly opens with the recorder ensemble attempting the FutureMinds corporate anthem, which was not written for recorders, or for children, or arguably for humans. The invoice is projected onto the hymn board where the harvest song used to go. The blazer man calls it an exciting learning journey; the deputy head mouths the words half a second late, like a hostage. Prudence raises her hand to report that slide two divides by zero, and is thanked for her enthusiasm.

choices.text: Sell the blazer man a list of difficult parents

outcomes.text: You quietly hand the blazer man a list of parents who ask questions, ranked by volume. He pays a finder's fee and then, delighted, a retention bonus to keep the list current. By Thursday the school gate has a guard hut, a barrier, and a sign about safeguarding that no child can read but every parent understands. The money is excellent and folds flat, unlike your posture when the apprentice teacher says good morning to you, twice, in case you missed it.

choices.text: Stand beside the teacher who stops singing

success.text: When the apprentice teacher lowers her recorder and refuses the second verse, you stand up next to her, which in assembly terms is a declaration of war. Row by row the singing dies until the anthem is one man in a blazer, alone, performing the harmonies of a corporation. The guards, with nothing to guard against except quiet, are sent home early. The deputy head marks the register afterwards and, against every instinct he owns, records the silence as full attendance.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_retaliation Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You stand when the apprentice teacher stops singing, and a guard mistakes your rising for a lunge. You are helped from the gymnasium through the small door, the one sized for children, at speed. The anthem finishes without you; the bruise is anthem shaped. Even so, the parents notice who stood first, and the biscuit tin appears on your doorstep that evening with one biscuit in it, which is how these people say thank you.

choices.text: Ask him to walk everyone through slide two

outcomes.text: You raise your hand, politely, and ask the blazer man to walk the assembly through slide two, the one with the division. He begins confidently, slows at the denominator, and dies somewhere in the footnote that cites itself. Four hundred children watch a grown man fail maths in a room where that has consequences. The guards look away, one of them mouthing the correct answer, and Prudence awards him a merit.

outcomes.text: You ask about slide two, and the blazer man pivots to a video called Our Learning Journey, loud enough to end questions as a category. The projector, technically school property, technically sold, drowns you out with a song about synergy performed by children who were paid in exposure. The assembly ends on schedule. Your question is logged as engagement, which FutureMinds counts as support.

text:
SCHOOL SALE

The morning post brings Invoice Two, which charges interest hourly and a fee for receiving the fee. The apprentice teacher has been given flexibility, which is the word FutureMinds uses for taking away her classroom and her chair. She teaches long division in the corridor now, chalk dust on everything she loves. The deputy head laminates the new fee schedule with the expression of a man laminating his own conscience.

choices.text: Carry benches until the corridor is a school

outcomes.text: You spend the morning carrying furniture, and by noon the corridor is a school again: benches from the chapel, a blackboard from somebody's parlor, chalk donated in fistfuls. The apprentice teacher teaches long division to a hallway so crowded the guards must queue to observe it, and queueing, they learn some long division. FutureMinds bills the corridor for occupancy; the corridor, having forty tenants now, splits the bill forty ways into nothing much. Someone chalks a small hand on your door, which is corridor for one of us.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You carry the first bench in alone, which is one person fewer than a bench requires. The corner takes you at knee height in front of the entire remedial reading group, who sound it out together. The apprentice teacher splints nothing, sprains nothing, but documents everything, because documentation is the only furniture FutureMinds respects. The corridor school opens a day late, with you seated at the front, elevated, as a teaching aid on load bearing.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: File the fee for receiving fees as usury

success.text: You file Invoice Two with the magistrate under the usury statutes, flagging the fee charged for receiving the fee. The magistrate reads it twice, once as law and once as literature, and rules that a charge may not bill its own arrival, whatever FutureMinds' notation implies. Hourly interest is suspended pending an audit. The blazer man calls it a technicality, which from him is the highest compliment a law can receive.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You file under the usury statutes and FutureMinds' advocate, whose lanyard is nicer than the magistrate's robes, reclassifies the fee as a subscription. Subscriptions, it turns out, may compound, recur, and grieve you, all lawfully. Costs are awarded against the corridor. The apprentice teacher adds the ruling to the maths syllabus under worked examples of getting away with it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Crack Invoice Two for a paying client

success.text: You take a private commission from a rival academy that wants to know how FutureMinds' billing works, strictly for admiration. Three nights with a candle and Invoice Two gives up its secret: the interest references a rate that does not exist, so every demand is a polite guess. Your client pays four coins, and you leave a copy of the working, anonymously, in the corridor's chalk box. The parents recognize the handwriting and say nothing, coldly.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You take the commission, crack the invoice, and make the mistake of mentioning your findings near the blazer man, who outbids everyone in the room within a minute. Eight coins, cash, and your working goes into a drawer that locks with a sound like a school closing. The corridor hears you were bought before you get back to it. The chalk box is moved so that you would have to ask where it is, and nobody waits to be asked.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Wednesday, the corridor, and the apprentice teacher reaches the end of her last textbook with half a term left. She looks at the pile of tuition invoices, and something in her snaps, quietly, in cursive. New homework, due Friday: solve your family's invoice, show your working, marks off for guessing. Forty children take out their pencils; Prudence asks whether she may use ink, and whether appendices are permitted. She is told yes, warily, to both.

choices.text: Post the mark scheme on the notice board

outcomes.text: You copy the mark scheme onto the corridor notice board, between netball trials and lost property: what the invoice claims, what it should sum to, and the space between, labeled profit. Parents read it at pickup, then merchants, then, fatally, a schools inspector who was only there about the netball. The inspector copies it into an official notebook, which makes it evidence with a border drawn around it. The deputy head laminates it before anyone can be ordered not to.
effects: schedule 5 r18_school_sale_crisis Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You post the mark scheme and by morning FutureMinds has glazed the entire notice board and declared its contents proprietary curriculum. A cease letter arrives addressed to The Corridor, Occupant Of. The netball trials are cancelled as collateral damage, which costs you more local goodwill than the maths ever earned. Behind the glass the mark scheme is at least preserved, like a saint, or an insect.
effects: schedule 5 r18_school_sale_crisis Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Sell answer keys to nervous parents

outcomes.text: You let it be known, at the gate, at pickup, that answer keys exist for nervous families, priced by anxiety. Business is immediate and excellent: eight coins by Thursday, six more for a rush order from a father who cannot face being corrected by his own daughter. The keys are accurate, your conscience is itemized, and the corridor pretends not to see you making change out of a pencil case. It works. That is the whole thing about it: it works.

choices.text: Buy the pencils and the long paper yourself

outcomes.text: You spend three coins on pencils, rulers, and the long paper, because invoice arithmetic does not fit on pages meant for spelling. The working, taped end to end, runs the length of the corridor and turns the corner by the trophy case. Parents who came to collect children stay to hold the tape. By Friday the corridor has a paper spine down its middle, and everyone steps over it the way you step over something holy.

outcomes.text: You arrive with forty rulers under one arm just as the gate guards decide stationery requires inspection. The inspection is conducted at shoulder height, briefly through you. You keep hold of the rulers, losing a little skin to the gravel, and the corridor gets its supplies dented but countable. Prudence signs for them, notes the damage in the margin, and adds, in ink, delivered with valor.

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Friday's homework arrives in wheelbarrows. Forty children have shown their working, and the working all shows the same thing: the invoice only balances if the school was sold for nothing, to itself, twice. Prudence's answer runs to nine pages and ends with the words see appendix. The blazer man declares a surprise fire drill, the kind where guards collect exercise books instead of children. The deputy head goes and stands in the doorway of the store cupboard where the homework is hidden, and does not move.

choices.text: Name a price for the appendix

outcomes.text: You take the blazer man aside and name a number for the appendix, and he doubles it before you finish, which tells you what it is worth and that you asked low. Fourteen coins in a school envelope, poster paint smell and all, and Prudence's nine pages leave the cupboard under his coat. On Monday you learn what a methodical child does with a Friday deadline: she numbered the copies. His is copy six of eleven, and copy one is already somewhere with a wax seal on it.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_compromise Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Fill the cupboard doorway with coats and biscuits

outcomes.text: You arrange the parents across the cupboard doorway in good coats, unhurried, holding biscuits, the least chargeable formation known to law. The guards ask everyone to move along and are offered a biscuit instead, and there is no procedure for that; the drill manual assumes hostility and has nothing on hospitality. The fire drill ends when the fire fails to attend. The homework stays hidden, and one guard leaves his helmet on the pegs by the small door, ostensibly by accident.

outcomes.text: You brace the cupboard door and the wheelbarrow takes the brunt when the guards test their mandate. In the scrum you catch a corner of shelving with your ribs, filed under minor abrasions, resented in full. The homework survives; the biscuits are confiscated as evidence of premeditated assembly. The word premeditated does a lot of work in that report, none of it honest.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_compromise Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Declare the homework protected coursework

success.text: You invoke the Examinations Charter, coursework clause, which vests every marked page in the child who wrote it. The guards, being unable to mark, cannot own; being unable to own, cannot seize; the logic takes four minutes and the blazer man ages a year per minute. The homework is declared coursework, protected, sacred, filed. Somewhere in the store cupboard, forty exercise books become the best defended documents in the region.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_compromise Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You invoke the Examinations Charter and FutureMinds produces an amendment, dated yesterday, notarized somehow, defining the invoice as curriculum and all responses to it as company property. The magistrate on duty is a graduate of a FutureMinds night course and recuses himself into agreeing. The cupboard is sealed with tape that says EXCITING. The tape is the worst part, everyone agrees.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_compromise Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

text:
SCHOOL SALE

A settlement arrives on paper so good it apologizes for the rest of the week. FutureMinds will enroll every child in a Scholarship of Perpetual Gratitude, repayable in adulthood at rates to be advised, and there will be a signing ceremony with a bell choir and a banner that says PARTNERSHIP. The apprentice teacher reads the terms aloud in the corridor, slowly, the way she reads back spelling mistakes. Prudence calculates that a scholar would make the final payment at the age of ninety one, assuming good health and no further education, and posts her working next to the netball scores.

choices.text: Throw a rival prize giving in the bike shed

outcomes.text: You hold a rival ceremony in the bike shed at the same hour: certificates for Best Working, a choir of parents who cannot sing but mean it, and a banner reading NO THANK YOU in a child's confident lettering. Attendance at the official signing collapses, because the shed has cake. The bell choir drifts over, then the guards, then, at the very end, the deputy head, who claims he is checking the fire exits and stays for two slices. Nothing gets signed anywhere, which was the entire syllabus.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You hold the rival ceremony and the bike shed, built for bikes, objects to hosting forty families. A roof panel resigns onto your shoulder mid speech. The certificates are handed out anyway, slightly bent, while you sit on an upturned crate being seen to with the first aid kit from another century. FutureMinds' ceremony still fails; yours merely bleeds a little, which the parents agree is the better kind of failure.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Volunteer to read the scholarship aloud

outcomes.text: You volunteer to give the ceremonial reading, and nobody at FutureMinds checks what you plan to read, because volunteering looks like surrender. You read the scholarship, all of it, aloud, at pageant speed, with the bell choir instinctively tolling behind the worst clauses. By the repayment schedule the front row is weeping, and not in the sponsored way. The banner is down before the buffet, folded by the same guards who hung it, with the care of men who suspect banners now.

outcomes.text: You take the lectern and get through two clauses before the sound is cut and the choir is cued to drown the rest in bells. The official minutes record a moving address, speaker unknown, content lost. FutureMinds declares the ceremony a triumph in a newsletter nobody can unsubscribe from. Your name goes onto a list titled Disruptions, which is at least alphabetized.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Unbolt the signing table and walk it out

success.text: You wait for the choir's loud bit, unbolt the signing table, and walk it out through the fire exit with two parents who used to move pianos. A ceremony cannot survive the loss of its furniture; contracts, like assemblies, need somewhere flat. The guards escalate to a posture the region will discuss for weeks, but by then the table is in the bike shed, serving as cake stand to a better party. The blazer man signs nothing all day, and a man like that, unsigned, is just a blazer; the parents cheer the result and still vote, gently, that next time you ask first.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You get the table two bolts loose before discovering it is oak, ceremonial oak, the kind of oak with opinions. The guards find you underneath it, which saves them the trouble of putting you there. Three days of bed rest, one formal caution, and a line in the incident book reading furniture, attempted. The signing goes ahead, thinly attended, over your horizontal objection.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Late on Sunday the deputy head unlocks the laminating room and shows you what he has been keeping warm: the original deed of sale, and stapled behind it, Prudence's appendix, copy one of eleven, marked in his red pen at nine and a half out of ten. Read together they prove what everyone felt and nobody could write down: FutureMinds paid for the school with the school itself, pledged as security against its own purchase price. The academy bought nothing, from nobody, and has been invoicing the town ever since for the privilege of watching. The half mark, he explains, was for untidy margins, and he has been dreading her appeal all week.

choices.text: Enter the homework as evidence, ink and all

outcomes.text: You lodge the deed and the appendix with the tribunal as a single exhibit, homework and evidence stapled into one genre. The tribunal's auditor checks the working for two hours, finds nothing to correct, and asks, in writing, whether the author is available to check his. The sale is voided from the first signature: a school cannot be its own price. Outside, the parents who came to wait begin teaching each other the appendix on the courthouse steps, because apparently that is what this town does now.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_legacy Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You lodge the exhibit and FutureMinds' advocates request an adjournment to consult their experts, who are their accountants, who are the problem. The tribunal grants it, plus a gag on discussing proprietary arithmetic in public. The corridor spends the recess memorizing the appendix instead, since memory is not discussion. Prudence approves this reading of the gag order, which is the nearest thing to a merit the law has earned all term.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_legacy Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Sell FutureMinds advance notice of the folder

outcomes.text: You sell the blazer man advance notice of the folder, timed, priced, exact. He pays fourteen coins without haggling, which should have told you something, and orders an entire cabinet shredded, the wrong one, at speed. The tribunal date holds; the folder was copied weeks ago by a nine year old with a numbering system, and you have been paid handsomely to change nothing, which is the purest job the System offers. The biscuit tin does not visit your doorstep for a long while after that.

choices.text: March the folder to the tribunal in a crowd

success.text: You walk the folder to the tribunal at the center of a procession: parents in their good coats, the corridor school in single file, the recorder ensemble playing something that was probably once a hymn. The folder rides flat on a borrowed lectern, carried like a relic. Nobody touches it; there are too many witnesses to count, so the guards count them instead, which keeps everyone usefully employed. The tribunal takes delivery on the steps, and the town applauds paperwork, sincerely, possibly for the first time.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_legacy Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You walk the folder over in procession, and on the courthouse steps a hired hand makes a grab that mostly gets your collarbone. The folder travels the last stretch overhead, passed hand to hand above the crowd, faster than anyone could have run with it. Delivery is accepted; your arm goes in a sling that three children sign before you can object. The clerk lists you in the record as conveyance, injured, and the town decides that is your title now.

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Term ends with a prize giving in a gymnasium that belongs, on paper, to the people sitting in it, held in common trust by the parents and apprentice teachers, with a rota for everything including joy. The apprentice teacher has a classroom, a chair, and a plaque reading HEAD OF ARITHMETIC, ACTUAL. By the door hangs Invoice Two, laminated, graded, wearing one gold star, studied every year the way other schools study the bones of extinct predators. The deputy head laminates freely now, for love. Prudence holds a seat on the timetable committee that nobody remembers voting on; she simply attended, and minutes exist to prove it.

choices.text: Charge admission to see the famous invoice

outcomes.text: You set up a small table by the door and charge admission to view the famous invoice: a coin for townsfolk, three for rival academies, five for anyone in a blazer. The queue is constant; inspectors travel from three regions to stand before it and feel things. Fourteen coins by week's end, and the school never asks for a cut, which somehow makes it worse and also does not stop you. The invoice, laminated, watches you make change, and if it objects, it keeps that in an appendix.

The invoice, laminated, watches you make change, and if it objects, it keeps that in an appendix.

choices.text: Accept the seat Prudence saved you

success.text: You take the seat Prudence saved you on the timetable committee, between a parent who bakes and a parent who audits, the school's two natural resources. The work is small and endless: rotas, roofs, the quiet arithmetic of who needs what. When the next glossy acquisition letter arrives, the drills pay off; a first year spots the compound interest by the second paragraph and receives a merit before lunch. The invoice on the wall has done its teaching, which is more than FutureMinds ever managed at any price.

fail.text: You take the committee seat and your first assigned duty is playground supervision, which sounds gentle and is not. A dodgeball, thrown with syllabus level precision, finds you behind the ear on day one. The incident form runs two pages; Prudence countersigns as witness and includes a diagram with angles. You are voted most improved supervisor at the next prize giving, a certificate the apprentice teacher laminates herself, tenderly, while you hold the ice.

choices.text: Bill FutureMinds in their own notation

outcomes.text: You send FutureMinds an invoice for educational services rendered: one demonstration of arithmetic, forty pupils, materials provided by the customer, expressed entirely in their own notation. Legally they must either pay it or argue in public that the notation means nothing, and their advocates bill them a fortune to explain the trap slowly. They pay. The school frames the receipt beside Invoice Two, predator and lesson hung at the same height, and the deputy head dusts them both on Fridays, evenhandedly.

You send FutureMinds an invoice in their own notation and it comes back marked insufficient postage, unpaid, with a promotional leaflet for their newest venture, an academy of financial literacy. The corridor laughs for a full day, which is worth something, though not legal tender. The leaflet goes up beside Invoice Two as a companion exhibit. Prudence grades it in the margin: two out of ten, shows no working, see me.

text:
FOREST COMMONS

At the edge of patience. the forest villages' assembly discovers that Royal Timber Destiny has made ancestral woods converted into inventory official policy. The dispute centers on the old paths. a tree cited for unlawful standing has been appointed to keep the process dignified.

text:
FOREST COMMONS

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

Eleven at night, and the barkeep is dusting the tab wall, four generations of chalk debts nobody alive remembers drinking. A brass calculator sits on the bar beside a cheese sandwich, and it has just printed a slip demanding the sandwich post collateral. The deacon of arrears arrives at closing time with word from Saint Compound Interest: the inherited tabs come due at the next full ledger, principal plus everything since. The tavern debt circle looks at you the way people look at someone who can read.

choices.text: Copy the tab wall into the deacon's ledger, at his rates

outcomes.text: The deacon pays eight on the spot and six when you finish, and never once checks your spelling, which tells you plenty about how the totals got this way. You copy four generations of chalk into his portable ledger while the calculator watches, printing a small slip that says NOTED. The circle does not say anything. They just stop saving you a stool near the fire.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

outcomes.text: The deacon pays eight on the spot and six when you finish, and never once checks your spelling, which tells you plenty about how the totals got this way. You copy four generations of chalk into his portable ledger while the calculator watches, printing a small slip that says NOTED. The circle does not say anything. They just stop saving you a stool near the fire.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Pool every receipt and memory the circle can produce

success.text: You push three tables together and the circle empties its pockets: receipts, promissory scraps, a betrothal contract with a bar tab in the margin. By midnight you can prove at least a third of the wall was paid twice, once in coin and once in a pig. The barkeep chalks your name in the corner reserved for people the house trusts with the books. The calculator prints a slip asking whether the pig posted collateral.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

fail.text: The pooling goes well until two families discover their great-grandfathers are both recorded buying the same round, and the argument re-enacts a fistfight from ninety years ago with you in the historic middle. You take an elbow meant for a man who died before your mother was born. Still, once the swelling settles, the circle counts you as one of theirs. Inherited debts, inherited bruises.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Demand the original instrument be read aloud, in full

outcomes.text: The deacon unrolls the original instrument and gets four words in before discovering the founding entry is water damaged, beer damaged, and possibly bitten. He is required by his own procedures to read only what is legible, which comes to roughly a preposition and half a date. The room takes minutes. The calculator prints a slip that says INCONCLUSIVE, which from a collections instrument is nearly an apology.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

outcomes.text: The deacon reads beautifully, from memory, in the cadence of a man who has buried this parish in installments. It turns out the instrument survives in triplicate at the shrine, certified, laminated, and cross-referenced against baptismal records. You have accomplished a dramatic reading of your neighbors' obligations. The calculator adds a line item for candle depreciation during the performance.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

Midnight in the back room, the account books open on the pool table like a body at a wake. The oldest debtor lights a candle for every drink her great-grandfather may not have finished, which takes a while. The calculator has raised its demand: the sandwich now owes interest on itself, compounding hourly. Somebody has to speak to the debts directly.

choices.text: Call the roll of the dead and let their tabs answer

success.text: You read the wall name by name, and for each one somebody stands and says what they know: he finished the drink, she never ordered it, that round was poured for a wake and wakes are on the house by a custom older than the saint. Debt by debt, the ghosts acquire defense counsel. The oldest debtor claims eleven ancestors and disputes nine of them on the merits. The calculator prints slips all night and files them under TESTIMONY, a category it invents around two in the morning.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_retaliation The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

fail.text: The roll call raises more than testimony. Around the fourth generation the room goes cold, a tankard slides itself down the bar, and the re-enacted quarrel of two dead regulars borrows living fists, one of which finds you. You are struck by a punch thrown in the previous century. The circle apologizes on behalf of its ancestors and stands you a compress.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_retaliation The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Audit the seventh column by candlelight

outcomes.text: You audit the seventh column and find where a clerk carried a two while drunk, in the year of the long winter, and every total since has been standing on that two like a church on a bad foundation. Corrected, the wall shrinks by a third before anyone has argued anything. The calculator recalculates, hesitates, and prints a slip that says WELL. The deacon will hate this, which the room agrees is a point in its favor.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_retaliation The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

outcomes.text: Your candlelight audit uncovers three errors, all of them in the parish's favor, all of them now conspicuously discovered in front of witnesses. The total goes up by a barrel and a half. The oldest debtor suggests, kindly, that you stop helping. The calculator prints a slip that says THANK YOU, which is somehow worse.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_retaliation The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Charge admission to the seance

outcomes.text: You set a stool by the door and charge admission, two coins to hear the ledger read, three to hear your own ancestors in it, and the parish pays, because everyone wants to know what grandfather really drank. Fourteen coins by midnight. The circle notes that you have found a way to make the debts pay you, which is the saint's own business model, minus the vestments. The calculator prints you a receipt without being asked, professional courtesy.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_retaliation The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

Dawn finds a schedule of distraint nailed to the tavern door, naming stools, tankards, the dartboard, and one sandwich, cheese, collateral status pending. The deacon has deputized the calculator, which now prints seizure slips with a little drawing of a hand on them. Saint Compound Interest, the notice explains, forgives nothing, that being the miracle he is patron of. The barkeep is holding the sandwich like a hostage negotiation.

choices.text: Serve the shrine a counterschedule of the saint's own tab

success.text: You serve it at the shrine door: forty years of feast day rounds poured on the house, sacramental wine advanced on credit, one christening barrel never invoiced out of respect. By the saint's own tariff, unpaid hospitality compounds. The deacon reads the counterschedule twice and goes the color of skimmed milk. The calculator, consulted as a neutral party, prints a slip that says HE OWES, and the parish sleeps better than it has in a generation.
effects: schedule 4 r18_debt_jubilee_coalition The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

fail.text: The deacon accepts your counterschedule graciously, stamps it, and files it, which you learn is the worst thing that can happen to a document. Filing incurs a fee. The fee incurs interest. By his arithmetic your rescue attempt is now itself on the wall, a small tab of your very own, the first new debt chalked in years. The circle appreciates the gesture and winces at the price.
effects: schedule 4 r18_debt_jubilee_coalition The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Go through the account books alone before dawn

success.text: Alone with the account books, you find two things: an uncashed promissory note from a horse trader, still good, worth four coins at the counting house, and a founding entry whose ink runs the wrong way across the page, as if written from the far side. You pocket the coins and the question. The circle is cool with you for handling the books without a witness, which is fair. The calculator watched the whole time and prints nothing, which feels like a kept secret.
effects: schedule 4 r18_debt_jubilee_coalition The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

fail.text: Alone with the books, you find a page of eighteenth-generation interest calculations so ornate that a collector of antique debts pays eight coins for it, framed by suppertime. The gap in the ledger is noticed by noon. Nobody can prove it was you, and everybody knows it was you, and the deacon cites the missing page as grounds to estimate, which is the most expensive verb in his vocabulary. The calculator prints a slip that just says SEEN.

choices.text: Spirit every named heirloom into friendly kitchens

outcomes.text: By nightfall every item on the schedule of distraint is somewhere else: the dartboard behind the baker's flour sacks, the tankards christened as flower pots, the sandwich moving between houses on a rota like a small cheese-based head of state. The bailiffs arrive to seize furniture and find a tavern furnished entirely with things too new to owe anything. The calculator, left behind as the only remaining asset, prints a slip that says ALONE, and the barkeep, against policy, pats it.

outcomes.text: The evacuation goes fine until the dartboard, whose owner never agreed to the plan, and whose owner's opinion arrives with his fists. You take a hit protecting a piece of cork that has been throwing off scores since the coronation. The heirlooms get hidden anyway. The bruise is entered nowhere, which by local standards makes it a gift.

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

Market day, and the deacon is drilling six hired bailiffs outside, teaching them to march in single entry. Word spreads that every wall in the parish holds somebody's grandfather in chalk, the baker's, the ferryman's, the bathhouse's. The calculator has begun addressing the sandwich formally, as esteemed counterparty. The barkeep proposes a supper for everyone who owes the dead.

choices.text: Sell the bailiffs a map of the parish's tab walls

outcomes.text: You draw the bailiffs a map: the baker's wall, the ferryman's post, the bathhouse ledger, every chalk graveyard in the parish, priced at fourteen coins for accuracy. They pay without haggling, which mercenaries only do when someone else's money is coming. The deacon commends your initiative in writing. The letter is the kind you fold small and hope nobody ever finds, and the calculator prints a copy for its records anyway.
effects: schedule 5 r18_debt_jubilee_crisis The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Host a supper for everyone who owes the dead

outcomes.text: You buy the bread, three coins of it, and forty debtors of the dead eat at one long table, comparing walls the way old soldiers compare scars. It emerges that half the parish's ghosts drank in the same rounds, which makes the debts less like separate nooses and more like one very long shared umbrella. The bailiffs peer in the window at a room with nothing worth seizing and too many witnesses to try. Two of them look hungry. The barkeep sends bread out anyway, and the calculator prints a slip it later claims was a misprint: FEED THEM.
effects: schedule 5 r18_debt_jubilee_crisis The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

outcomes.text: The supper feeds forty and scalds one, you, wrist to elbow, when the soup kettle takes exception to being lifted by the handle that was always loose. The parish debtors eat together anyway, and the evening counts as a success by every measure except your forearm. The oldest debtor dresses the burn with the confidence of a woman who has treated four generations of kitchen injuries. The bailiffs outside conclude the singing means reinforcements.
effects: schedule 5 r18_debt_jubilee_crisis The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Read the bailiffs the contracts they signed

outcomes.text: You invite the bailiffs in for a drink, on the house, and read them the pay contracts the deacon had them sign. Clause nine advances their wages as a loan against wages, at the saint's usual rate, meaning every week they work they owe slightly more than they earn. Two quit on the spot. One asks, quietly, whether the circle takes new faces. The calculator prints their severance math for free, and the deacon finds himself drilling three men and a formation gap.

outcomes.text: You read the bailiffs their contracts, and they listen, and then the deacon appears in the doorway with amended contracts, pre-signed, the amendments dated yesterday. Retroactive paperwork is the saint's oldest miracle. The bailiffs shrug back into formation, a little sadder, no freer. The calculator prints a slip that says NICE TRY, and you cannot tell whose side it meant it for.
effects: schedule 5 r18_debt_jubilee_crisis The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

Feast night, and the deacon sets the account books on the bar for the closing of generations. The calculator is asked for the grand total and prints, and prints, and the paper slip runs down the bar, out the door, and partway up the street. Somewhere in the fourth generation of interest the number stops meaning money and becomes a kind of weather. The sandwich, still uncollateralized, sits in the middle of it like the eye of the storm.

choices.text: Answer the grand total as one tab, all names jointly

outcomes.text: The circle answers as one tab: every living debtor steps forward and takes joint custody of the whole monstrous number, all names, one obligation. The deacon flips through his forms and finds no line for a debt owed by everybody, since a debt owed by everybody is functionally weather, and the saint does not bill the rain. Filing is suspended pending clarification. The calculator prints a slip that says HM, its longest pause on record.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_compromise The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

outcomes.text: The circle stands together all night, and standing together all night is how the ferryman's widow faints at the fourth hour, onto you, and you onto a stool, and the stool onto history. Something in your shoulder files a grievance. The count is unfinished at dawn, the deacon claims the interruption as a concession, and the room disagrees loudly enough to wake the street.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_compromise The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Object that the total exceeds the definition of a debt

success.text: You raise a definitional objection: the tariff defines a debt as a recoverable sum, recovery is bounded by the worth of the parish, and the total on the slip exceeds the parish, the county, and by the fourth generation, plausibly the concept of counting. Whatever the slip is, it is not legally a debt. It may be scripture, it may be weather, it is not collectible. The deacon requests a recess. The calculator prints a slip that says FAIR.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_compromise The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

fail.text: The deacon hears your objection and produces appendix twelve, in which the saint's actuaries define the parish as including all future parishioners, their heirs, and their heirs' custom at the bar. The debt is thus recoverable from people not yet born, which balances beautifully and horrifies everyone. You have taught the ledger about posterity. The calculator prints a slip that says OH NO.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_compromise The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Take the deacon's fee to measure and certify the slip

outcomes.text: The deacon needs the total slip measured and certified for the shrine's records, and pays by the yard, and it is a great many yards. You walk the paper up the street with a counting wheel while the parish watches from doorways. Fourteen coins for certifying the exact size of your neighbors' misfortune. The calculator prints your invoice unprompted, itemized, and does not editorialize, which stings more than if it had.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_compromise The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

Noon, and the deacon returns wearing the smile of a man with a settlement in his coat. Terms: every inherited tab forgiven, in exchange for the deed to the tavern, custody of the account books, and delivery of one sandwich, cheese, unencumbered. The calculator reads the offer and prints a slip that says DO NOT, its first editorial. The circle wants to know since when does a saint want a sandwich.

choices.text: Make the deacon define unencumbered for the record

outcomes.text: You ask the deacon to define unencumbered for the record, and the record obliges: the sandwich is under an active collateral demand from a licensed calculating instrument, and has been for weeks. Encumbered, therefore undeliverable, therefore the offer fails its own conditions. The deacon glares at the calculator. The calculator prints a slip that says TERMS ARE TERMS, and the settlement dies of its own fine print, which the room agrees is the loveliest death available.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_reversal The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

outcomes.text: You raise the encumbrance point, and the deacon smiles wider and produces a definitions rider: for purposes of this offer, sandwich means any bread-adjacent asset, encumbered or not, and the tavern has a pantry full of those. Your objection is noted, annexed, and consumed. The offer stands, hungrier than before. The calculator prints a slip that says THAT IS NOT WHAT SANDWICH MEANS, its first known italics.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_reversal The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Walk the deacon out by his vestments

success.text: You take the deacon by the vestments and escort him to the door with the gentle firmness of a last call. The room does not cheer, exactly, but the room does not stop you either. His bailiffs, three men and a vacancy, consider intervening, consider their contracts, and stay put. The circle wishes you had let the paperwork do it, manhandling clergy being bad for appeals, but the settlement leaves in his coat and does not come back.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_reversal The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

fail.text: You reach for his collar and discover the deacon has done this before: he steps aside, and the remaining bailiffs have not. You wake up under the pool table with the account books for a pillow and three fewer things unbruised. The deacon left with his dignity, which is worse than leaving with the tavern. The calculator prints a slip that says GET UP, which is the closest it comes to bedside manner.
effects: schedule 3 r18_debt_jubilee_reversal The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Put the terms to the whole room at once

outcomes.text: You read the terms to the whole room and put the question, and the room answers the way a wall falls, all at once and no single stone responsible. Nobody trades the tavern, the books, or the sandwich, in that order of outrage, the sandwich placing surprisingly high. The deacon writes down declined without asking anyone's name, because there is no one name to write. The calculator prints a slip that says SO SAY ALL, and the barkeep pins it over the till.

outcomes.text: The room debates the terms with democratic thoroughness, which is to say a stool leaves the floor by the second hour. You intercept it with your ribs on behalf of due process. The offer still dies, but untidily, and the deacon departs describing the scene in language that will not flatter the parish upstream. The oldest debtor notes that at least nobody sold anything.

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

Three nights later the oldest debtor empties her receipt tin onto the bar, and there it is: the founding entry, held to a candle, reads backwards because it was written from the other side of the page. The first tab was the house owing the drinkers, a victory round promised in writing and never poured. Every generation of interest since has been compounding in the wrong direction. The calculator checks the arithmetic twice and prints a slip with nothing on it but a question mark.

choices.text: Sell the deacon the backwards page, cash first

outcomes.text: The deacon pays fourteen coins for the backwards page and burns it in the shrine brazier before the wax cools, the fastest transaction of his career. He does not know the oldest debtor made three copies before supper, on the principle that anything worth keeping is worth keeping twice more. So you are paid, the page survives, and the only thing actually destroyed is the assumption that you would not sell it. The calculator prints a slip that says KEPT RECEIPTS, and it had.
effects: schedule 4 r18_debt_jubilee_legacy The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Carry the page to every wall it ever compounded on

success.text: You carry the backwards page wall to wall, baker to bathhouse, holding it up to candles so every debtor of the dead can watch the founding debt change direction. The parish spends a whole evening doing arithmetic for pleasure, which nobody thought they would live to see. Whole walls recalculate toward zero and keep going. The calculator accompanies you on a cushion, prints running totals at each stop, and is introduced by the barkeep as counsel.
effects: schedule 4 r18_debt_jubilee_legacy The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

fail.text: Halfway through your circuit of the parish walls you step on the total slip, still lying in the street from feast night, and its yards of paper take your feet the way ice takes a cart. You land on the arithmetic. The page survives because you held it aloft while going down, which the witnesses agree was the correct priority. The bruise on your hip is shaped, the oldest debtor claims, like a percentage sign.
effects: schedule 4 r18_debt_jubilee_legacy The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Move for symmetry: credits compound too

outcomes.text: You enter the page in evidence and move for symmetry: if debts compound down the generations, credits must compound up them, at the saint's own published rate, from the night of the unpoured round to now. The shrine's actuaries run the numbers three times and request a recess of indefinite length. By their own tariff, Saint Compound Interest owes this parish a sum best described as devotional. The calculator prints the figure, then prints a slip that says SAINTS PAY RETAIL.
effects: schedule 4 r18_debt_jubilee_legacy The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

outcomes.text: The deacon hears your symmetry motion and cites the exclusion of ascending interest, appendix nine: obligations flow downhill, like water and blame. Credits do not compound, they merely age. Your page is genuine, admitted, and worth exactly its weight in paper by his arithmetic. The room learns the ledger was never a scale, only a funnel, and the calculator prints a slip that says APPENDIX NINE IS WRONG, unsigned, deniable, and pinned to the wall by morning.

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

A quiet Thursday, and nobody remembers whose idea it was to simply give the calculator what it asked for. The barkeep sets the sandwich on the intake tray, collateral at last, and the machine goes silent for a full minute, which for a calculator is prayer. Then it prints one word per tab, ABSOLVED, ABSOLVED, ABSOLVED, until the chalk wall is more paper than slate. The deacon arrives at closing time to find no ledger left to close.

choices.text: Rechalk the wall as a menu of forgiven debts

success.text: You chalk the new wall together: HOUSE OF FORGIVEN DEBTS, and beneath it every absolved tab, reordered as a menu, prices listed in stories. The ferryman's round, ninety years standing, costs the tale of the night the river froze; the betrothal tab costs the whole scandal, told properly, with the pig. Strangers come in to order debts and leave having paid in full. The barkeep says the wall finally earns its keep, and the calculator, retired to a shelf of honor, prints one slip a night, ABSOLVED, in case anyone forgets what the house serves.

fail.text: You chalk the top row from a borrowed ladder, and the ladder has opinions, and you meet the floor with the word ABSOLVED half finished above you. The oldest debtor completes the lettering while you count your elbows. The menu opens that night anyway, debts priced in stories, your fall already among the dearer items on it. The calculator prints a slip that says CASUALTY, ONE, FORGIVEN, and the barkeep frames it.

choices.text: Draft the machine's absolution as binding precedent

outcomes.text: You draft it as an instrument: whereas the collateral demanded was tendered, cheese and all, the demanding instrument declares all antecedent obligations satisfied in perpetuity, witnessed, dated, and printed in the machine's own hand. The System accepts it for filing, there being no form on which to refuse a document that ends things. The deacon is reassigned upstream, and the shrine quietly amends the saint's patronage to include debts nobody remembers taking on. The calculator signs with a slip that says PAID IN SANDWICH, and the phrase enters the local law reports.

You draft the absolution in your best legalese, and the System files it under catering, there being a sandwich in the operative clause. Legally the parish has not been forgiven, it has been served lunch. The deacon appeals on those grounds and is told the appeal window for lunch is thirty minutes and has closed. The wall stays absolved by pure administrative confusion, which the barkeep says is how most miracles actually arrive. The calculator prints a slip that says CLOSE ENOUGH.

choices.text: Charge pilgrims to see the machine that forgave a parish

outcomes.text: You charge two coins at the door to see the machine that forgave a parish, and they come from three counties over, and they pay. Fourteen coins the first week alone. The circle mutters that forgiveness should not have a gift shop, but the calculator has never printed for a larger audience and shows no sign of minding. Each visitor receives a slip of their own: ABSOLVED, IN PRINCIPLE, PENDING SANDWICH. Several report feeling genuinely lighter, which nobody budgeted for.

text:
OPEN PRISON

During a routine emergency. the families at the prison gate discovers that Corrections Growth Group has made a jail expanding faster than the town official policy. The dispute centers on the cell keys. a rehabilitation rack with adjustable guilt has been appointed to keep the process dignified.

OPEN PRISON

text:
STRIKE PRESS

Eleven at night, and the composing room has one job left in it. The printers walked out at noon; the Concern's morning edition does not mention this, but it does carry a new editorial, hand delivered by the Night Editor for immediate setting: INK TAKES NO SIDES. The backwards compositor reads it upside down in the galley, twice, and says it gets worse in both directions. Somebody has to decide what happens between midnight and the five o'clock run, because that is four hours in which these presses belong to nobody who is watching.

choices.text: Invoke the Concern's own corrections policy

outcomes.text: You take the strike's absence upstairs as an error of fact: the Concern corrects all errors of fact, page two, small type, and the walkout of its own printers is a fact currently in error by omission. The Night Editor consults the policy he wrote, finds no exemption for facts he dislikes, and grants the strike two column inches beside the bread prices. The editorial still runs, but now it runs beside evidence, and the backwards compositor says that is the worst place an editorial can stand.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_assembly Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_assembly Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

outcomes.text: The Night Editor hears you out, then produces the corrections policy's final clause: the editor decides what is a fact. He thanks you for your interest in accuracy and adds a line to the editorial saying reports of a strike are themselves a kind of weather. By morning the paper has printed that its printers are all present, which the printers, reading it outside in the cold, find educational.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_assembly Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Take the rush fee to set the editorial tonight

outcomes.text: The Night Editor pays double for overnight hands and does not ask where your sympathies sleep; you set INK TAKES NO SIDES in fourteen point while the crew watches through the window. He tips on top of the fee, because loyalty bought at night costs extra and he likes a receipt. The piece runs page one, handsome as a headstone, and a gentleman of the Owners' Circle sends a note praising the paper's steadiness in trying times. The backwards compositor finds your name in the day book and turns it upside down.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_assembly Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Stay for the midnight run of a second paper

success.text: Between midnight and five the presses belong to whoever oils them, and tonight that is the crew, printing four pages of everything the Concern declined to notice under a new masthead: The Late Correction. You pull the first sheet yourself, still warm, same fonts, same ink, opposite facts. The Smudge runs bundles to the early carts and comes back grinning like the safe was open the whole time. By dawn both papers exist, and only one of them is surprised.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_assembly Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

fail.text: The first run of a secret newspaper is mostly things going wrong quietly. You catch a dropped forme with your shin at two in the morning, which keeps the type sacred and your dignity in escrow, and the crew agrees the noise you made can be logged as an alarm test. The Correction still prints, four pages, slightly crooked, and the backwards compositor says crooked is honest for a first issue. The watchman notices lights and is told the presses are being cleaned, which is even true from a certain distance.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_assembly Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

text:
STRIKE PRESS

Two in the morning, issue three, and the layout argument has reached the stone. The backwards compositor wants the Correction to reprint the Concern's editorial word for word, same type, set flush against the strike report it insists cannot exist. The Smudge calls this stealing; she calls it quoting at length with intent. The five o'clock deadline is not participating in the debate.

choices.text: Deliver both papers and collect twice

outcomes.text: You take over the morning route with a bag on each shoulder: the Concern for the doorsteps, the Correction for the same doorsteps, a modest fee from each masthead for the service. Subscribers pay for one paper and receive a controversy, which most of them consider a bargain and some of them shout about. The Owners' Circle notices circulation rising and congratulates the Concern on winning an argument it has not noticed it is in. The crew notices your bag money and starts a ledger with your name at the top, underlined in a way you decide not to interpret.
effects: schedule 5 r18_strike_press_retaliation Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Hold the forme while the page goes together

success.text: The reprint works better than either paper deserves. Editorial on the left insisting there is no strike, strike report on the right in the identical voice, and readers can feel the two columns disagreeing through the paper. She locks the chase, you carry it like a full tray of drinks, and the run comes off clean at four. In the morning the Concern's own subscribers ask the Concern why it argues with itself, which is the correct question one issue early.
effects: schedule 5 r18_strike_press_retaliation Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

fail.text: A forme full of locked type weighs what a small argument weighs, and at three in the morning you lose the argument. It goes down on the stone, you go down after it, and pied type sprays across the floor like the alphabet giving notice. The crew resets the page from memory while your knuckles swell, and the backwards compositor says every paper worth reading has cost somebody a hand somewhere. Issue three runs late, which in this trade is a kind of bleeding.
effects: schedule 5 r18_strike_press_retaliation Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Set the two columns so they answer each other

outcomes.text: You interleave them line for line down the page: the editorial states, the report replies, the editorial insists, the report produces a name and a shift roster. Nobody wrote the dialogue and the dialogue is devastating; it reads like the Concern interviewing itself under oath. Two magistrates and a bishop are seen buying the Correction with their own money. The Night Editor reportedly reads it twice, once as an editor and once as a casualty.
effects: schedule 5 r18_strike_press_retaliation Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

outcomes.text: The interleaving is clever the way a lock is clever: most people just see a door that will not open. Readers write in asking whether the printers have pied the whole paper, and one subscriber requests the old format on medical grounds. The crew runs the plain layout at four, and she tells you the joke was good, the timing was good, and the audience was a newspaper reader at breakfast. You file the idea under later, where the best tricks wait.
effects: schedule 5 r18_strike_press_retaliation Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

text:
STRIKE PRESS

The Concern's answer arrives in two parts. Page two carries a formal correction stating that the Concern remains neutral and that, additionally, no second newspaper exists; the press hall now carries four hired watchmen with lanterns and a rota. The midnight window is shut, the Smudge recites the new guard schedule like bad news set to music, and issue four is due to a public that officially cannot be reading it.

choices.text: Smuggle the set pages out to a borrowed hand press

outcomes.text: The pages leave the building inside bread trays, lead type under warm loaves, which is the only time the Concern's watchmen have ever held a door open for the opposition. The pie shop's back room holds a hand press old enough to remember better arguments, and the crew works it in shifts with flour on their sleeves. Issue four smells faintly of crust and sells out by nine. The pie woman takes payment in review pages, having always wanted her gravy assessed in print.
effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_coalition Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_coalition Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

outcomes.text: A bread tray full of lead is a truthful weight and the bottom knows it. It gives out in the alley, type across the cobbles, and you take the tray's corner in the ribs holding the crash away from the good galley. The watchmen hear everything and find nothing, because the crew scoops an alphabet out of the dark in ninety seconds flat while you breathe in commas. Issue four comes out a day late with a note apologizing for events, which are not specified, which sells papers.
effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_coalition Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Read the watchmen their own contract aloud

success.text: The contract hires them to guard the presses against interference with printing. You stand on a crate and establish, clause by clause, that the night crew are printers, printing, and that the only interference on the premises is therefore whoever stops them. The head watchman reads it twice, decides the pay is the same either way, and spends the shift holding his lantern where the compositor needs light. The Concern has hired security for its rival, at rates it negotiated itself.
effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_coalition Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

fail.text: The head watchman listens to your reading with real appreciation and then explains his own theory of contracts, which is that the man who pays is right. He walks you to the gate with genuine courtesy, wishing you luck in the print trade. Word travels that the crew tried lawyering the locks off, and the Night Editor adds a triumphant paragraph to the standing correction. The window stays shut, and now it knows why.
effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_coalition Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Go through the Night Editor's spike after hours

success.text: The spike behind his desk holds every story the Concern declined to run, filed neat as regrets. You copy the three best, walk them to the Correction, and take the finder's fee the crew votes you with slightly narrowed eyes, freelancing being a word they pronounce carefully. The spiked stories run under the headline WHAT NEUTRALITY DECLINED, and one of them concerns the watchmen's wages, which the watchmen also read. Two lanterns fail to report the following night.
effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_coalition Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

fail.text: The spike also holds a story about the Owners' Circle, and the Owners' Circle pays handsomely for it to stay on the nail. You leave with a heavy purse and light footsteps, and the story goes back on its spike like a coat that fits somebody else. The crew never learns exactly what happened, but crews can smell an exactly. Your name in the ledger acquires a second underline.
effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_coalition Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

text:
STRIKE PRESS

Issue seven opens a new front: the review. The Concern has assessed the Correction as a scurrilous pamphlet with surprisingly professional kerning; the Correction has reviewed the Concern's editorial as literature, calling INK TAKES NO SIDES a promising early work of fantasy let down by its research. Circulation of both papers is climbing, since the public has worked out you need the full pair to follow the fight. The backwards compositor says this is the first war in history where both sides bill the audience.

choices.text: Publish a review of the Concern's review

outcomes.text: Your review of their review runs six paragraphs and awards the Concern's prose full marks for confidence, none for load bearing. You note that a paper reviewing its rival has conceded the rival exists, contradicting its own standing correction, and you cite that correction by date and page. The Concern must now either correct the correction or review you back, and it does both in one issue, which the Correction reviews. Somewhere in the loop, neutrality quietly files for retirement.
effects: schedule 3 r18_strike_press_crisis Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

outcomes.text: The review of the review of the review loses the room somewhere around the third mirror. Readers begin writing in to ask who is winning, and one asks, with respect, whether both papers are secretly the same paper, which lands closer than anyone prints. The Night Editor seizes the opening to declare the whole exchange beneath the Concern, from the top of page one. She tells you the trick was sound, but the trade has a saying: never set more mirrors than you can dust.
effects: schedule 3 r18_strike_press_crisis Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Sell each editor the other's page proofs

outcomes.text: The Night Editor pays for tomorrow's Correction; the crew's makeup man pays for tomorrow's Concern; neither asks how, and both use the word discreet. For a week you are the only person in the city reading both papers a day early, a position with a salary from each direction. The papers, forewarned, sharpen against each other into their best issues yet, so you can argue you have improved the press by betraying it evenly. The Owners' Circle hears there is somebody who can get anything out of any building, and writes the name down.
effects: schedule 3 r18_strike_press_crisis Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Put your own coin in for the newsprint run

outcomes.text: The paper merchant, finding one address ordering stock for two feuding mastheads, develops nerves and wants coin up front. You put yours in the kitty without being asked, which in this trade is louder than a speech. The reels arrive by handcart at dusk, the crew stacks them like ammunition, and the pie woman sends up dinner on the strength of the smell of a good argument coming. Issue eight prints on paper that six people own a piece of, and reads like it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_strike_press_crisis Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

outcomes.text: A full newsprint reel outweighs a compositor and has no politics either, as you learn when one comes off the handcart on the slope. You get a shoulder under it, which is brave, which is the medical term for what happens to the shoulder. The reel survives, the street gets an anecdote, and the crew stands you the night's beer while forgetting your share of the cost. The merchant watches all of this and quietly doubles his nerves.
effects: schedule 3 r18_strike_press_crisis Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

text:
STRIKE PRESS

Disaster attends the morning edition, wearing gloves. The Concern seized the whole night to starve out the Correction, ran the presses till dawn, and printed thirty thousand copies of a front page set entirely in mirror writing, editorial and all: the backwards compositor did the emergency makeup at speed, and her hands set it the only way they pray. The Night Editor is calling it sabotage; she is calling it legibility for the deserving. Thirty thousand readers are holding the news up to shaving mirrors, and word is spreading that it works.

choices.text: Name your price for the emergency re-set

outcomes.text: The Night Editor needs the page rebuilt before the evening carts, and there is exactly one crew in the city that can set his fonts, a market condition you describe to him gently, with numbers. He pays your figure, then pays it again for silence about paying it, and the Owners' Circle wires him funds with a note about standing firm against extortion, some of which is you. The corrected edition makes the carts; the watchmen get reinforced out of the same envelope. The mirrored copies sell above cover price as curiosities, so everyone in this sentence is profiting except neutrality.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_compromise Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

outcomes.text: The Night Editor needs the page rebuilt before the evening carts, and there is exactly one crew in the city that can set his fonts, a market condition you describe to him gently, with numbers. He pays your figure, then pays it again for silence about paying it, and the Owners' Circle wires him funds with a note about standing firm against extortion, some of which is you. The corrected edition makes the carts; the watchmen get reinforced out of the same envelope. The mirrored copies sell above cover price as curiosities, so everyone in this sentence is profiting except neutrality.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_compromise Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Trade the crew's fix for the midnight window back

outcomes.text: The crew offers terms through the pie shop: a full re-set by noon, done properly, in exchange for the return of midnight to five and the watchmen stood down to a lantern apiece. The Night Editor, staring at thirty thousand backwards editorials, discovers flexibility. The re-set makes the evening carts and the Correction resumes lawful residence of the dark hours, if lawful is the word for a thing the agreement declines to mention. She corrects the mirrored page herself and sets one word of it backwards anyway, for the connoisseurs.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_compromise Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

outcomes.text: The crew offers terms through the pie shop: a full re-set by noon, done properly, in exchange for the return of midnight to five and the watchmen stood down to a lantern apiece. The Night Editor, staring at thirty thousand backwards editorials, discovers flexibility. The re-set makes the evening carts and the Correction resumes lawful residence of the dark hours, if lawful is the word for a thing the agreement declines to mention. She corrects the mirrored page herself and sets one word of it backwards anyway, for the connoisseurs.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_compromise Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

outcomes.text: The re-set is agreed, then the Concern's floor manager decides midnight is negotiable after all, and the handover becomes a shoving argument at the delivery gate. You end up between a bundle cart and a doorpost, briefly, in the tradition of peacemakers everywhere. The re-set happens because the trade is the trade, but the window comes back grudging and half guarded, and everyone logs the shove for later. The mirrored edition keeps selling; the public has taken a side, and it is the shaving mirror's.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_compromise Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

choices.text: Argue sabotage law over the mirrored page

success.text: The Concern's brief argues destruction of property; you exhibit the property, thirty thousand copies, selling. The magistrate holds page one up to the court mirror, reads it clean through, and rules that a newspaper printed backwards is still a newspaper, merely one that has chosen its readers with unusual care. Damages require damage, and the ledger shows a profit. The Concern pays costs, and the ruling is reported accurately in one paper and neutrally in the other.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_compromise Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

fail.text: The magistrate turns the mirrored page over twice and rules for tidiness, which is where rulings go when nobody reads the exhibits. An injunction lands on all unauthorized night printing, worded broadly enough to cover dreams. The crew appeals, which takes money, which takes time, which is the point of it. She spends the recess setting the injunction backwards for the next issue, so at least the deserving can read it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_strike_press_compromise Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

text:
STRIKE PRESS

Terms arrive on Concern letterhead, which is how you know they are losing. The proposal: the Correction may print openly, evenings, on the house presses, provided it submits each issue for a neutrality reading by the Night Editor. The crew reads this aloud to general laughter, then reads it again slower, because inside the insult there is a real offer: daylight, and the presses, in writing. The pie shop hosts the counter-drafting; the pie woman charges by the clause.

choices.text: Hold the crew together through the tempting terms

outcomes.text: The Concern's drafters have scattered sweeteners through the clauses, a raise here, a named chair there, addressed to individual pockets. You get everyone to read their bribes aloud in turn, which converts them from temptations into entertainment. The crew answers as one hand: strike the neutrality reading, keep the daylight, and the offers stand together or nothing does. The Concern, bargaining against people who laugh in rota, concedes the reading and pretends it was a typographical error.
effects: schedule 5 r18_strike_press_reversal Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing
